name: Disc Jam
review:
I tried the game while it was/is free for the weekend. I have played about 8 hours non-stop and am loving the competitive side of it. Hopefully the developers take the ranking system seriously and professionally and I could see this game being a solid 1V1/2V2 competitive game.

The game is extremly fast paced and can be played easily by anyone, but requires fast reactions and a lot of game knowledge to master the art.

With all this being said, there are a couple of cons to be mentioned.
The first and most annoying thing to most PC players is the controlls. I use an Xbox controller so the controls work well for me but I have read a fair amount of complaints about the issue.
The game skins are a bit gimicky and its quite cartoonish.

Otherwise this game is great fun. I play on Oceanic servers in australia and seem to be fine from any lag issues.

Disc Jam is a fun game but not a game I would spend money on. It has a cool concept and as far as I am concerned is pretty unique for a video game. In Disc Jam you will find that goals are scored alot so when you have to respawn to the middle everytime you score it is quite annoying overtime. I think this could easily be fixe to improve the overall flow of gameplay. Another big problem of Disc Jam is the keyboard controls, or what is supposed to be keyboard controls... Don't play this game if you don't have a controller. Period.

Overall it is a fun game that I never would of known about if it wasn't for a free weekend, thanks to the devs for letting me try this fun little game but personally I do not think it fits the price tag as it lacks alot where content should be, it only comes with - one map, four characters, one game mode. I personally  hope in the future this game will become f2p as I think most other people would agree 15 bucks is far too much for this little content.

name: Disc Jam
review:
Disc Jam is a fantastic fast paced sports-like game. It is extremely easy to pick up, but hard to master and get all the intricacies to the game. 

The game has gotten significantly better since I started playing it in Pre-Alpha, receving a new character, as well as tons of new cosmetics. Dedicated servers were added, which made connections between EU and US-E much better, and the recent addition of cross-play has truly made this game more enjoyable. Pre cross-play it was hard to find a match because the game's playerbase has been very small. But I highly doubt anyone should have issues finding people now that we have the ability to matchmake into PS4 players.

The Pros:
-Fast paced and highly addictive gameplay
-Easy to pick up mechanics
-Support for local multiplayer
-Just all around an extremely fun game

The game does have a couple cons to it though:
-Only 1 map to play on as of this writing
-The tutorials can be a bit ambiguous and somewhat hard to execute, needs some re-work.
-Not a vast amount of cosmetics. There are a lot, but definitely needs moar!
-Music can get quite repetitive after a short time, of course you can always just turn it off and play your own music.

Overall though, I've played my heart out on this game and even with some of it's imperfections, namely the 1 map part, even with that I've put over 300 hours into this game and have still not found it to get stale. 

10/10 would Jam again.

name: Disc Jam
review:
Since release, this game has added dedicated servers (enabling 2v2 matchmaking) and cross-platform play. I went from being unable to play with some of my friends through peer-to-peer to being able to find matches consistently within minutes.

Mechanically, the game is incredibly fun and challenging. The push and pull of momentum in a match completely trumps any tennis or sports game I have ever played, thanks in part to the Juiced mechanic, which boosts your throw speed after three successive perfect throws. But don't miss a beat or you'll lose it! Maintaining your Juiced state while trying to make your opponent drop his is just half the fun. 

Landing perfect throws is precise and feels great, blocking and charging your Super is satisfying and returning opponents Supers, though tricky, feels amazing. Not to mention you can throw lobs, perform Wall Skips and even return an opponents Super with your own if you're fast enough. Super throws can also stack with your Juiced state. There's plenty of options in how to get an advantage but you always have the tools to counter them. 

Whilst there's only a few characters and currently one arena, the game feels like a cohesive whole as it is. High Horse clearly focused on having fun and engaging gameplay first and foremost. They also have more content planned but the fact that they focused on implementing dedicated servers and cross-platform play as soon as possible shows thier priorities are in order. I would even say it's above and beyond what should be expected for such a small studio. Kudos, HH.

name: Disc Jam
review:
Apparently this game had issues with the low player count. Well, the game just got the crossplay update and I'm glad to announce it is working perfectly! I get matches within seconds in EU+NA.

About the game itself, well, as someone who played Windjammers when it was out on the arcades I find everything very familiar. I only had to adjust to a couple things like the perspective, the fact that you can get hit in the back if you don't face the right direction and the need to have a fully charged shield to get a super shot. Otherwise I'm just sliding and curving my disc as I was doing back in the 90s.

The game could use more characters and a little more choice for super shots, but that's not really aggravating. A couple things I would really change though:
- make the hitbox of the disc bigger when it bounces off to the ground - many times you're clearly below the disc yet you can't catch it
- lower the startup on super shield or remove it entirely so that you have more chances for super shots. You basically can't get a single one unless they throw a disc at you in a straight line.

It is a pretty fun multiplayer game once you get into it. Just beware that there are no single player modes whatsoever.

name: Disc Jam
review:
Game is great, but also dead. Waited several minutes for every queue and by several I mean like 5-20 minutes. I don't think there's anything wrong with matchmaking.  I think there just isn't anyone playing. 

Mechanics are a little overcomplicated if you're coming from Windjammers but it's not hard to learn. 
Windjammers: one button to dash, knockup the disc, and throw. With a second to lob.
Disc Jam: Throw button is block, have to charge the block to knock disc up for super. Lob button is also dash (which I think should be the other way around at least). 

There's an awkward delay after dashing to grab the disc so you can't get rid of it right away like you could in Windjammers. 

Curving is pretty easy in this. You can do slight left and right curves with one button press, or you can hit throw and rotate the controller or pad in quarter or half circle to curve it more. You don't rotate controller then hit throw. You gotta hit throw then rotate. 

There's only 4 goofy looking characters with minor stat differences and all 4 can have the same super moves. 

The bad cam angle makes it difficult to judge the disc path. I think it would be nice if they gave us the option of playing with a top down angle. 

If the game wasn't dead I would be playing it like a fanatic even with the few shortcomings, but with no community the game is pointless. Might as well stick with Crazy Power Disc or Windjammers for local multiplayer.

Another thing I preffered with Windjammers and Power Disc is that you could volley almost indefinitely no matter how fast the disc gets. In this the playing field is quite large and it's easy to circumvent  your opponent.

name: Disc Jam
review:
Alright, so here's the deal.
This game is EXTREMELY fun for me to play, and when playing the beta, i had loads of fun playing with others and playing with my brother in custom matches. I played quite a bit, and even though i didn't get as much time as i would have liked during the beta, this game easily fell into my top 10 favorite games of all time at that time. When i heard the game was releasing soon, i was excited because i thought the playerbase would get bigger, because it was fairly hard to find many people in a game (eg. finding the same person 3 times within 5 games) and i thought the release would come with lots of publicity and more people to play with.
Unfortunately, my predictions were completely wrong, because with the release came a dead game that is now abandoned by many players. Once i bought the game, I went straight into it, expecting at least a few people to easily find and then jump into the game I had loved. But low and behold, i sat for over 10 minutes trying to find a game, with literally nobody joining. I was very confused and sad, because I had loved this game, played a lot of it, gained different skins, discs, and taunts, met many people who were fun to play with and were friendly, and i got pretty good at it. I kept persisting, but never found any games with anyone to play with.
Even with new additions of characters, skins, taunts, poses, discs, names, emblems, integration of deticated servers, crossplay with pc and ps4 (also its apparently free on ps4), ranked stats, and "many more features to come", the game is an absolutely dissapointment when it comes to the community. Because there is none.
I VERY MUCH want to give this game a recommendation, and if anyone reading this wants to re-ignite the community spark that was once in this game, be my guest and buy this game, if you have any friends to play it with, or if you would like to try to contribute to keeping the community alive. It is very, very fun and is easy to get into and hard to master. But for the time being, i cannot give this game a recommendation rating, because i spent 15$ on this game and found that it was a waste of my time and money, because nobody seems to care about or has forgotten about this game. A piece of advice for the devs; try lower price, more publicity, and more content (i know you guys are working hard, but i dont know if it's enough) and MAYBE your game will grow substantially. I do think that those who seem to think the game seems fun and those that wish to buy this game should. But be warned that you are most likely buying a dead game with no player base.

I truly hope this game can once again regain it's charm, and bring back the playerbase so that everyone can enjoy this.

TL;DR: Very fun, but no playerbase, basically a dead game. Buy if you want, expect nobody to find in matchmaking.

name: Disc Jam
review:
** Edited to recommended **

This is a perfect example of a game that should've been Early Access. The price was too much for what you got, I think it's fine now.

The game is cool. You play 1v1 or 2v2 frisbee on steroids. Some might remember Windjammers from the past. It's essentially the same. You can dash around and do special throws with street fighter like inputs. Four different characters have their own values for speed, throw strength, etc and you can unlock costumes, tags, yadda yadda.

The only thing you can do is play online or practice throwing the disc at a wall. With a patch they added support for dedicated servers. Also, crossplay with PlayStation users is planned and hopefully arrives sooner than later. Meanwhile a system for skillbased matchmaking is in play and gets constantly tweaked and improved.

The game is fun. Bought it with a friend and we have a blast playing 2v2. There are issues though and this needs a bit more polish. But they are working on it. Many people compare it to Rocket League, feels more like SARPBC for now. I recommend it for the potential and the effort already done. There is a constant stream of updates and improverments.

tl;dr
+ windjammers
+ online/couch coop
+ fast skillbased game
+ short matches
- prize
- no real offline "vs bots" play

However, I feel that the game doesn't deliver enough, it's MP only and it can take a while to find a match at times on an EU server, and when I do, the ping is above 150 and nearly unplayable. If they introduced a singleplayer mode, say challenges or AI, then I can get onboard with that.

name: Disc Jam
review:
The rightful successor of Windjammers, Disc Jam uses its mechanics and adds more. You have a shield to deflect the disc and you can charge it to use a Super throw if the disc is slow enough. If you throw right away the disc you get a boost and it can happen 3 times, 4th time you can use a Super. Of course multiply this with all the different curves you can create, the lob to throw the disc on the ground,  quick curves, dashes etc  and you can really outsmart your opponent. Mindgames ensues.
Very rewarding arcade game, suitable to play 5mn or 2h, you can play single or 2v2 games, 2v2 local online, 2v2 online with a friend etc. 
If you just want to play localy, you have to go to offline and everything will be unlocked by default, which is nice to have fun right away and for each player to have a personnal color and such.

The game is a little barebone as I write this, you have 4 characters, each slightly different, 1 map, 2 musics, ~10 colors (haven't counted), ~8 taunts and victory poses (haven't counted again), something like 50 Titles and 50 Icons. So you can really customize your character and player profile.
You get all of this by playing which will make you earn money(even in loss), which you can use to the prize machine to randomly get an item. There are colors and rarity. If you get an item that you already have you sell it for 500 coins ( each usage of the prize machine costs 1000 coins).
Ranked matches, leaderboards and statistics are coming soon, crossplay will follow with the PS4 online.

Talking about online it's very good, seems to be P2P, you can see your ping at any moment and can narrow your search to lower pings.  I find games in 10/20 seconds, it can take more time depending of the number of players.
On PC you have a chat box to write what you want, even though you have very little time in game to talk between rounds, that's why the devs have added a quick message feature like in rocket league using the Dpad.

Overrall the experience is very enjoyable, the mechanics are simple to understand yet hard to master with a great skill curve (not talking about the mechanic which is also called skill curve haha) like Windjammers has. (the arcade game it is based on).
The devs are following the game very very closely and are updating/adding things weekly. It has a great future ahead and I think it deserves to be bought even right now.

name: Disc Jam
review:
Welcome to Disc Jam! The competitive sport you never knew was your absolute favorite. A Frisbee disc/Action game hybrid.... but this isn’t just ultimate Frisbee, so put your putters away. This is Pong on steroids all ramped up with special moves, dives, and lobs shots! THIS is the vision Windjammers strived to be years ago, but revamped with the technology of the Unreal4 Engine.

The goal of Disc Jam is simple. You take 2 players (or 2v2) on a court similar to a tennis court, and take turns throwing a frisbee at each other in an attempt to get it past your opponent to score some points. First to 50 wins a set, and a match is best of 3 sets. The gameplay is fast and frantic with diving, lob-shots, special moves, curve shots, guard plays and an arsonal of tactics and mechanics to keep each player guessing and keeping each new game fresh. And it’s always crazy when you throw out a special move, and your opponent catches it and shoots a special right back your way. I especially recommend this game for adrenaline junkies. There is always something exciting going down, and as soon as the match is over you just want that rush again. With frantically fast gameplay, easy co-op to comp, loads of unlockables, and  simple-to-learn-but-arduous-to-master style, you and your friends are going to have limitless amount of good times.  I give Disc Jam a well deserved 8/10, with room to grow

Lots of cosmetics already, without any kind of microtransaction (my wallet thanks you so much for that), and more contents yet to come.

Easy to play, yet harder to master, with potential for high skill plays. Mastering dem perfect curves is gonna take some practice.

This game full potential is yet to be unleashed, but really, what a potential... Can't wait!

name: Disc Jam
review:
Disc Jam is a solid competitive game, and can be a lot of fun to play! It's easy to pick up, and hard to master. There are a number of characters, skins, emblems, name tags, and discs to choose from. However, as of right now, the amount of content is pretty limited.

Here's why:
After going through the basic and advanced tutorials, if you're playing alone, there is nothing to do except for free play, and customising your character. The playerbase is small meaning it can take a very long time to find a match online. There is no option to enable bots for offline play, and, in doubles, teams are required to be balanced regardless.

Do I recommend Disc Jam? Definitely yes if you have others to play with. If you're playing alone, then it can get a little boring after a while. That's mostly because this game is underrated and needs a bigger playerbase.

It's still a new game so hopefully the devs can add some new features in future updates.

name: Disc Jam
review:
OK, so anyone over the age of 30 will know this really is basically the arcade game Wind Jammers brought to your home. Yes, i recommend it, but with some caveats.

Disc Jam has the potential to be a truly top game. The premise is there, the foundations are there. Now it just needs some extra floors and bells, and some very-much-needed tweaking.

See, as fun as the game is, it is also equally frustrating. There is so much emphasis on getting the "power shot" right, every time. The margin for error in pulling this off is effectively zero. Let me give you a great analogy. Go get yourself a stopwatch, start it, and stop it at precisely 1.00 seconds. Then do this again and stop it at exactly 2.00 seconds. No hundreths allowed. If you cannot do this 10 times in a row without failure, then you're going to be taking the path marked "Frustration".

Now combine the above ardour with a matchmaking system which currently pits you against the closest player geographically, regardless of skill. What has happened to me just about every time i've played is that i'm put up against someone who was in the beta, and has twitch-learnt the above ABSOLUTELY REQUIRED skill sometime during their hundred-hour playtime.

I don't want to take away from the amazing job the two (yes, 2) developers have done here. But the game definitely needs filled out with better matchmaking, alobby, more stadia, more forgiving power shots and a single player mode. Given how active the devs are, i've no doubt that all those features will come in due course.

This is more or less a modern reimagining of the Neo Geo arcade title "WindJammers".

If tennis, frisbee and 90's XTREMEism had a midnight encounter this would be their love child.

The title is terrific fun in nice, bite sized doses yet you may find yourself playing for hours.  It is simple to learn but with a deeper learning curve (a la Rocket League).  Very pure and arcadey fun that favours reflexes just as much as out-witting your opponent via positioning and tactics.

Highly recommended!

Just bought the game last night and have already clocked in almost 8 hours as of now.  Its just like Wind Jammers for anyone that is old enough to remember that game.   It's also the PC version of Air Hockey.   Its fun challenging and certainly a game ill enjoy playing for months or even years to come.

First thing I want anyone who buys this amazing game ($10 as of now).  Invest in a controller.  YOU NEED a controller.  I use my PS4 controller, but any will do.  Its extrememly difficult to play with just the keyboard.   

The lottery system works with its free currency, but to be completely honest I wish there was a cash shop to buy skins and such.  Maybe an alternate currency?  Overall for a simple game, its extrememly fun.   Match making can be interesting as to who you go up against.  I have smashed people who are obviously new to the game, and also been destroyed by people who have been playing since the Wind Jammer days.  Either way games are fast. Most are done in under 10 minutes. 

100% worth the money!!

name: Trials of the Blood Dragon
review:
"Hey, did you watch all of the [i]Far Cry 3: Blood Dragon[/i] cutscenes like we told you to?"
"Yes sir, Ubisoft."
"Which one looked the most... interesting to you?"
"The sex scene. Why?"
"Funny you say that, because we want you to make an entire [i]Trials[/i] game based on that cutscene."
"...I'm not sure we follow."
"Continue the [i]Blood Dragon[/i] storyline where it left off after Rex defeats Sloan and the Omega Force. Rex gets married with Dr. Darling and they have two kids. One of them is named after a The Police song. The other just has a made-up name. Do what you will with the boy's name, but in the year 2019, there is a fourth Vietnam War, and they are sent to put an end to it. Just like [i]FC3:BD[/i], it should be as 80s and over-the-top as possible."
"I'm still not sure we follow."
"Just do it, Redlynx. We're sure that this game will be just as good as [i]FC3:BD[/i] and will totally not be forgettable at all."
"...yes sir, Ubisoft."

That's the conversation I imagine happened for the development of this game, and boy, was Ubisoft wrong.

As stated before, [i]Trials of the Blood Dragon[/i] continues from [i]Far Cry 3: Blood Dragon[/i]'s story, where Roxanne and Slayter, Rex "Power" Colt and Dr. Elizabeth Darling's children, are sent to shoot up some alleged communists and end Vietnam War 4. Yeah, two 12-year-olds going to war. Some of the plot points are ludicrous even for a sequel to a game based on well-known 80s films. [spoiler]Examples of such are that Dr. Darling is actually a Blood Dragon taking the form of a human, which makes Rox and Slay half-Blood Dragon. Dr. Darling disguises herself as General Ryback to help her kids kill the "communists," because while they could be communists, they're still just other humans standing in the way of the Dragornians, and the Killstar actually corrupts Rex so that it swings him to communism. That, I can understand though, because the Killstar in [i]FC3:BD[/i] used health as ammunition.[/spoiler] I wasn't much of a fan of the Saturday morning cartoon art style they were going for either. They completely butchered the Blood Dragons with it.

The gameplay is a mixed bag. While the APC and on foot sections weren't the worst things ever, the bike and minecart physics never felt all that great. Oftentimes I felt like I had the right alignment with the bike to meet the ground again, only to do an upside-down wheelie and kill myself. Minecarts share the same issue, only with added weight. The levels with the grappling hook bike and jetpack were absolutely miserable. The grappling hook is fun when it's first introduced, but in later levels, you really need to channel your inner Spider-Man to get past them, especially on Triangulation. As for the jetpack, Rox/Slay really wants to go full throttle with it even with the slightest tap of the W key, which makes it harder to turn and easier to undershoot and overshoot everything. In Extermination, where you not only have to deal with bad jetpack controls, you also have to transport a fragile, highly explosive bomb-probe to the mother bug, which does not help at all. What does help, though, is a forgiving amount of checkpoints across all levels. Even if you're not dead, you can still respawn at a checkpoint at any time if you know you're about to, and the respawn time is instant, so you don't have to wait to try again.

I also enjoyed the references to other franchises sprinkled throughout the game, even if some weren't from the 80s. There were allusions to [i]Hotline Miami[/i], [i]Halo[/i], [i]Star Wars[/i], [i]Super Mario Bros.[/i], and even [i]My Little Pony[/i]. Power Glove also made an amazing soundtrack for this game, but do you expect anything less from one of the greatest synthwave artists of all time (and where is "Clutch (Dragon Mix)" in the album?)?

Overall, [i]Trials of the Blood Dragon[/i] is a D game. Even if the plot is ridiculous for an 80s-based game, it's still charming, but gets overshadowed by crappy gameplay and physics. It's not a great sequel to [i]Far Cry 3: Blood Dragon[/i] and it's definitely not the [i]Hotline Miami 3[/i] we've been waiting for. Hopefully we can go back to the FPS genre for the next [i]Blood Dragon[/i] game. [spoiler]Maybe we won't even get another [i] Blood Dragon [/i] game at all since Rex, Dr. Darling, Rox, and Slay all killed each other in the true ending.[/spoiler]

name: Trials of the Blood Dragon
review:
Blood Dragon infects another Ubisoft franchise.

Story: 

Rex Power Colt has settled and now has two kids Roxanne and Slayter. Just like their father 12 years before, they are tasked with a crazy task, take down the Empire of Vietnam and stop their forces and end Vietnam War 4. 

For fans of the original Blood Dragon, the story is about the same absolutely over the top and crazy experience, just a lot more toned down for a more younger audience, but the same type of humor is still here. I've seen people say that the new characters are a hit or miss, but I found them to be an enjoyable duo, although they do get on your nerves from time to time and I do think my man Rex is just the best. 

Graphics:

Visually Trials of the Blood Dragon oozes the same type of Neon barf that you expect when you hear the words Blood Dragon. Purple and Pink are the two primary colors and I have to say that I absolutely loved how vibrant all of the environments looked, my 80's aesthetic love has been pleased. 

A minor negative is that although there are vibrant colors, the lighting sometimes make certain environments look pretty rough, especially when darker lighting is introduced. It's sometimes even hard to see where you're supposed to go and it might end up getting you killed. 

Audio:

The developers knew that there was no point in not bringing back Power Glove and they came back with an absolute banger of a score. While the overall score isn't as memorable as the Far Cry Blood Dragon game, it's still filled with great and catchy synth tunes, that make the gameplay exhilarating. 

The voice acting is pretty decent overall, matches that saturday morning cartoon style very well and hearing some returning voice was a delight too. Sound effects for the bike and the explosions are also top tier and I wouldn't expect anything else from Trials. 

Gameplay:

Ever played a Trials game ? Well this is the same type of deal except now it's Blood Dragon themed. For those who are new, here's the rundown. Trials of the Blood Dragon is a physics based racing game where you're on a motocross bike and have to reach the end of the level as fast as possible without crashing. 

Trials of the Blood Dragon, when you play with the bike is an absolute blast and is just as good as when I originally used to play it when I was a kid. The controls are incredibly simple and the bike controls incredibly well, so when you're just blazing through each level, without crashing it's a blast.

However one of the newer additions of this particular game are the on stage segments and the different vehicle stages. Every once in a while you'll have to ditch the bike and do something with a different vehicle. While the actual on foot segments aren't particularly fun, they at least work and I had fun using the twitch shooter mechanics, however as soon as you get control of the jetpack the game becomes incredibly annoying. 

The controls of it are so finnicky and going fast isn't really an option because the controls are just so wonky. In one of the levels you also have a bomb attached to you and it's wobbling all over the place and I could never get the controls to work in my favor. It was just so annoying and all I would wish for is to get back on the bike. 

Verdict: 

Trials of the Blood Dragon is a bit wonky in areas, but when it's good it's great. I absolutely adore the style and I kind of wish Ubi gave out more weird spin offs and maybe that will happen one day, but as we stand today this is still a fun game and worth the time, just don't expect a Far Cry 3 Blood Dragon type experience.

Final Rating:
7.5/10

Pros:
+ Power Gloves score is amazing 
+ Great looking environments 
+ The bike levels are really fun 

Cons:
- Any levels that don't have the bike suck
- Lighting make certain areas look pretty bad 

If you liked this review please consider joining https://steamcommunity.com/groups/completingthebacklog and https://steamcommunity.com/groups/ImperialReviews

First of all, don't go into this expecting a pure Trials biking game since you only use the bike about a third of the time. The bike that you do get is pretty bottom tier as far as Trials bikes go, and the parts that don't involve the bike are a bit awkward and clumsy. However, the over the top nature of the game and the pure fun that can be had out of it makes up for most of these negatives since you can really see the experimentation in the track design and various abilities/modes of travel. No spoilers, but the story is kinda nuts, and it's a blast to see these wild tracks for the first time. 

I am recommending this for a casual playthrough, but not really for going for the A+ ranks on each track. This is because the goofy mechanics don't really lend themselves well for consistency and perfection, and you have to have some pretty solid runs to get the best rank. 

It's a lot of fun though if you put aside the fact that it's not really a traditional Trials game.

Trials of the Blood Dragon is Ubisoft's attempt to tie more games into the Blood Dragon "series" that Far Cry 3's famed DLC tried to introduce. It's wacky, filled with nonsense and anime and explosions, and most interestingly does everything within the Trials engine - which really shows off just how much can be done with the creation tools. 

But, as a game, it's a bit boring. It has a lot of non-motorcycle segments and focuses a lot on the wacky story, and I both didn't feel compelled to go for higher medals, nor finish the game. It's unfortunately just not what I play Trials for. 

I respect it, but I don't recommend it.

name: Trials of the Blood Dragon
review:
This game is the perfect example of experimentation over refinement. This review comes from a huge fan of Far Cry 3: Blood Dragon, and someone who has dabbled in and played a few of the Trails games in the past. Seeing as I loved FC3BD, and had played some of the Trials franchise, I figured I'd give this one a try. This game was something I had wanted to play for a long time now, but the mixed reviews scared me off from it at first. Luckily, I caught it on sale and...well I'm glad I did.

This game is filled to the brim with experimentation, and has several styles of game-play. It has biking sections (sometimes either with a gun or a grappling hook), truck driving sections (which there are very few of), jet-packing, boss fights, and even on foot gameplay. And what's absolutely absurd is that there are several different subcategories of these various play styles, which change up gameplay even more! My take on the gameplay in a short break-down:

Biking - Hit or miss, but it's mostly fine. The physics seem to have gotten worse from the previous Trials games, which screwed me over a few times, but it's not the worst controls I've ever had to play with. The grapling hook can be pretty jank at times, but I must admit that the gun gameplay merging with the speed of the bike was some pretty great stuff. The level design isn't bad for the most part though, and when the level design is at it's best, it can be fun to zip through these levels, especially with the game's great aesthetic.

Trucking - Probably my favorite, but the only reason being that I don't have to deal with the sloppy or finicky controls that the other playstyles offer.

Jet-packing - Have fun trying to get past lazer beams while flying in this thing.

Boss fights - There was one boss which I actually think was decently enjoyable to fight, but other than that, the bosses felt incredibly frustrating.

On foot - In a Trials game? Why? Just...why. The platforming is not only basic, but very unsatisfying. I don't quite know how to describe it, but it doesn't feel like your character is jumping; more so it feels like your character just levitates in the air as you hold the button, and it's one of things where you really have to experience it for yourself to see what I mean. Anytime I would get into one of these sections, I would roll my eyes and hope to get back to the next biking section.

Now onto the story (spoiler free). It's not that great to be honest, and everything feels so disjointed and hard to follow. Granted, FC3BD didn't exactly have A tier writing or anything, but the game had a very good sense of humor to it, and was at the very least able to keep cohesion all the way through. There were very few points throughout Trials of the Blood Dragon that actually made me laugh. There are multiple twists to this story, which was a pleasant surprise, but these twists have little to no foreshadowing whatsoever, even in spite of them making sense in context of events.

The soundtrack is just fantastic. It is done by Powerglove, the same people that did most if not all of the tracks from Far Cry 3 Blood Dragon. Their work was phenomenal in the previous game, and their work still shines bright neon here as well, but with this game they're really able to show off a lot more of their potential. With the constant fast and action-packed pace that this game tries to portray, they were able to do a lot more with what they had. What's interesting is that I could swear that my ear would pick up on some remixes from FC3BD here and there, which were more than welcome. While I perfer the more atmospheric sounds of FC3BD, this still certainly is by no means bad stuff.

Conclusion: I am VERY glad I got this game on sale. If you catch it on sale and you're a fan of the retro aesthetic and synth music, or if you just want a narrative sequel to FC3BD, then I'd consider giving it a play. It was nice to be able to play the game just to see what it was like, but more than likely the only thing related to it that I will be revisiting is the soundtrack on YouTube.

Okay here's the deal.

There are much better trials games and MUCH better platformer games.

If you haven't already extensively played and enjoyed previous trials games, leave and come back to this review when you have.

Okay now assuming you are a trials veteran, here is what to expect. This is coming from someone who has 150+ hours played.

The story mode will be completed in about 2-3 hours.
This consists of a collection of 26 levels, which are a mashup of some beginner tracks, a couple jetpack skill games, a couple minecart skill games, some trials tracks with a grappling hook, and some platforming levels with truly terrible controls/physics.

The game has a goofy stupid story, visually the levels are good, and the music is great.

Throw in some bonus "extreme" levels that are a bit more difficult.

There is no Track Central, this is essentially just a standalone dlc for trials fusion.

If you think the game doesn't offer enough challenge, just try and beat me on the leaderboards lol.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

Why do I have so many hours played you may ask?
Well a lot of the fun in the game for me was that this game came out during a content drought for the trials community, and I had a lot of fun going back and forth with different people in the community competing on the level leaderboards, finding new strategies, etc.

I would not expect anyone else buying the game at this point in time to get much more than maybe 6-8 hours of playtime out of it.

name: Trials of the Blood Dragon
review:
I'm not too familiar with the Trials and Far Cry games, so to me, this an all-new experience.  Take my opinion for what it's worth.

GAMEPLAY:  
--- Basically you control a pair of characters through obstacle courses either on bike, foot, tank, and other various means.  
--- All movement is in 2D, but the backgrounds and stages are 3D rendered.  
--- The goal is to navigate around the obstacles and get to the finish line in the shortest amount of time possible, after which you get graded on your performance. 
--- Memorization of the traps and stage layout are key to getting a good grade.
--- Controls are very simple, involving a button for acceleration, braking, and left/right directionals for tilting your vehicle (think Excitebike for the NES).  Similar controls for foot missions, but involve jumping/shooting/platform elements.  Simple to play, but tough to master.    
--- Lots of unlockables and hidden goals in certain stages.  
--- High replay here if your aim is to be the best and unlock everything the game has to offer.  Otherwise, expect 5 or so hours to finish your first time through.
---  Supports either keyboard or controller inputs.

GRAPHICS:  
--- Looks sharp and runs nicely on my system, which is 5 years old.  As usual, visit any game's Steam page to ensure your system can handle it.
--- Vibrant colors reminiscent of the 1980's to help capture that decade's feel.
--- As mentioned before, movement is 2D, though the camera angles may change somewhat depending on the stage.  
--- Intermission cutscenes between levels use a kind of cartoon-like animation with 80's inspired elements.

SOUND:
--- Lots of 80's inspired synthwave tunes to help set the tone.
--- Explosions and sound effects sound great on my surround sound setup.
--- The dialogue (and story) can seem a bit childish at times, but might erupt a chuckle or two depending on your sense of humor.

OTHER NOTES:
--- A UPlay account must be created and active while playing this game.
--- Certain DLC items can be unlocked by earning UPlay coins, which are acquired after performing certain achievements

FINAL VERDICT
--- I found this to be a lot fun for what it was.  I picked it up for less than $5, and couldn't be happier with my purchase.  
--- It's one of those game I'll definitely keep coming back to in order to improve my grades and get better times.  
--- If you know nothing about Far Cry or Trials, it's still a simple enough game to pick up and learn without feeling lost.
--- Recommended for those folks who love dirt bike racing games, platformers, explosions, and music/animation with a 1980's feel.

name: Trials of the Blood Dragon
review:
So glad I got this game. I really liked Far Cry Blood Dragon and this is a fantastic addition to the mini-franchise spinoff. The intro is hilarious and the game is full of parody and satire, perhaps more so than the Far Cry game. Just like Far Cry, it is full of epic action and you really feel like a badass when you finish a level with no faults and a good time. The perfect run is elusive yet the gameplay and controls are so fundamentally simple that it's addicting. Furthermore, there's no waiting to restart, it's instant. The art style and music are excellent, as you'd expect, but so are the visuals. It's surprisingly more demanding than I expected at 4K max settings, I get some dips below 60  FPS with a GTX 1060 6GB but overall it's moor than playable and looks decent - better than Hotline Miami at least! Speaking of which, one of the chapters seems to be a direct competitor / parody what with the on foot sections and enemies, level design, masks etc.
Sure, there are some gimmicky and annoying sections where you're not on a bike but when they do get it right, and you have a near-perfect run, the game shines. Leaderboards also allow for near-endless replayability.
I honestly don't understand how this game got rated so poorly.  Maybe it's just my kind of game, and this is coming from someone who doesn't follow the Trials series. If you want something satisfying, simple and that is pure skill (i.e. no RNG), even if the skill cap is fairly low, it's a fun game to hop on. The levels are just the right length in terms of the balance between difficulty and content: they're short enough to make you want to replay them and not get frustrated when you fail, but long enough to make it very difficult to get a "perfect" run.

name: Trials of the Blood Dragon
review:
Haven't played rising yet, but compared to all of the other Trials games this game is the worst one for many reasons. The main issues I had when playing through the game were the gimmicks, the difficulty, and the "story." I'll go into more detail about these issues below.

1. Gimmicks
For some reason they thought it would be fun to add gimmicks like minecart levels, guns, platforming, and grappling hooks. Some are alright, but most of them are complete garbage. For instance, the minecart stages tend to have this issue where even if you land on the ground perfectly, your cart may sometimes flip over anyways and lead to you losing. The biggest gripe I have is with the grappling hook. When you go to the Japan themed area of the game, it heavily relies on grappling hooks and it shows how annoying this gimmick is. Another good are in the game that is great at showing how flawed the gimmicks are is the Mars/Hell themed area since every level in the area has some gimmick which is the focus of the level.

2. Difficulty
Trials games are known for being difficult, but this game falls flat. Yes, it can be difficult at times, but usually it is due to a previously mentioned gimmick being used in the stage. Other than that, most stages are Easy to Medium in difficulty with maybe a piece of a track being a bit harder than the rest. There are 3 stages which show off the bad of this game pretty good. First is a stage where the gimmick is that you have to carry a bomb on a trailer attacked behind you without making it explode. That might seem annoying enough, but the second half of the stage has you carrying the ball while using a jetpack which is just a complete mess. The second stage is the final Japan themed stage where you have to "fight" the 4 ninja characters and my god it takes so much time for a single stage, so if you are going for an A or A+ rating good luck with that. Finally is the final stage, which is the most lackluster stage in the game. Basically, you drive up and down mountains with ease for 8 minutes with the occasional jump, and then for 2 more minutes you do some of the platform/shooting section which can have some cheap moments where you get shot by an enemy you couldn't see.

3. "Story"
I get it is a parody of the 80s or 90s or some crap, but most of it just seems to be "lol random funny xD" which is fine I suppose. The big issue though has to be the ending. The ending (and secret) ending rivals that of the campaign ending of CoD Ghosts in that when you complete it the game practically spits you in the face and gives you an unsatisfying ending. I then looked up the "secret" ending, which literally adds a few more seconds which just says fuck you to the players a bit more.

So yeah, this game is hot garbage full of poorly made gimmicks which I hope never reappear in the Trials games. Maybe get it when on sale for dirt cheap if you really love trials and want it in your collection, but even then I highly recommend just ignoring this game.

name: Trials of the Blood Dragon
review:
First off, I've played Far Cry 3: Blood Dragon and all of the Trials games.

The story is just not believable, not even to a 10 year old kid. There are a few jokes here and there but none of them are very funny.

If you're expecting a game like Trials Evolution or Trials Fusion, you'd most likely be disappointed. The game-play is just not what you would expect from a Trials game. Half of the time you'll be playing as one of the kids in a 2D 'run and shoot' game and other half, you'll be playing in a 2.5D mode in Trials game-play with a few twists, I'm trying not to spoil too much. If you're a fan of the previous Trials games, then chances are that you won't be liking it at all.

The blood dragon story was totally unnecessary. There were several blood dragons in FC3:BD but in this game, there's just one? It's more like trying to kill a Godzilla. The story would be much believable if the developers had replaced the whole blood dragon concept with another monster and had left FC3:BD out of this game completely. After you unlock some of the hardest levels, you'll probably need a controller if you hope to complete them.

Whether you're a Trials fan or a FC3:BD fan or both, I wouldn't recommend this game to you. This game is a complete disaster.

name: Trials of the Blood Dragon
review:
[h1] The Rundown [/h1]
After the events of Far Cry 3: Blood Dragon (amazing game, check it out), Rex Power Colt has had two kids: Slayter and Roxanne, who have been tasked with dismantling the Empire of Vietnam, ending Vietnam War IV. You commandeer different vehicles depending on the course, and sometimes even complete missions on foot as either character. Just like Blood Dragon, it tries to pull off an OTT 80s style.

[h1] The pros [/h1]

[list]
 [*] The soundtrack in this game is great (but this could be my synthwave bias talking)
 [*] Tries to keep the Trials feel of difficulty in the motorbike levels.
[/list]

[h1] The cons [/h1]

[list]
 [*] Some of the jetpack levels are silver surfer tier difficult.
 [*] On foot is kind of clunky. While I didn't have much issue, I can see where others are coming from.
[/list]

[h1] The verdict [/h1]
In my opinion, the game needs LOTS of work. The aesthetic and soundtrack are fine, but controls for jetpacks are unbearable, and for the standard player, probably a put-off.
As a result, I [b] cannot recommend [/b] this game, sorry.

name: Trials of the Blood Dragon
review:
Trials of the Blood Dragon has a lot in common with its infamous E3 presentation; a great, bold, different idea on paper that turned out terrible in execution.

I came into this game expecting very polished level design as seen in the previous Trials games. Trials HD and Trials Evolution both had a great difficulty curve, tons of customization, and different bikes for you to choose from. This game offers none of that. You are set in a 27 mission single player experience. No different bikes. No customization. No user created content. No multiplayer. For the same price point as Trials Evolution, this game is an absolute joke when it comes to content. I was able to complete the *entirety* of the game in under 2 hours.

Plot: 

The idea of an actual intertwined story that links the missions together, rather than levels being discrete and separated from one another is a great idea. I don't know if I really needed a story within a Trials campaign, but the idea is great. However the execution on this story was awful. The plot is neither funny nor interesting like that of Far Cry: Blood Dragon. The two main characters have very stilted dialogue and poor voice acting.

Mechanics: 

There are three new mechanics added to this game over previous entries in the Trials series. Enemies and weapons, a grappling hook, and... on foot platforming.

The ability to use an smg on your bike is by far the best mechanic added. In order to complete races as quickly as possible, the smg allows you clear a path by shooting enemies, or by shooting switches in order to open doors. This provides a more flexible level design, and seems like a very smooth addition. The grappling hook functions in the same way as the gun, but you use it to reach certain vertical areas. I feel like this addition to the game was not as well executed as the machine gun's, as it removes any and all challenging platforming sections that were available in previous games.

Finally... on foot sections. God... What do I even say. This was an absolute mistake to add to the game. Clunky, slow, repetitive platforming that is not satisfying and NOT what I would want out of either a Trials game or a Far Cry game. One weapon. One enemy type. Not enjoyable in the slightest.

Final Verdict:

In theory, a cross between Far Cry Blood Dragon and the Trials series sounds like a wonderous time. Gun toting, action packed biking. Instead, we get an incredibly short, repetitive, unchallenging, and not too fun game that's mediocre at best.

name: Trials of the Blood Dragon
review:
PROS:
- A lot of variety in gameplay
- Interesting level designs and changes
- Story has its funny moments
- Great Set Pieces

CONS:
- Short as all hell
- Pretty easy compared to other Trails games
- Voice acting is BAAAAAAD

I was kind of surprised to see this pop up on steam, I honestly thought it was one of those shitty rip-offs that slide in through greenlight. Turns out this is all legit and once you actually start playing the game that becomes pretty obvious. The production values for the levels are way ahead of any of the previous Trials games. With most of them featuring at least one flashy set piece.

Of course the downside of that is that you end up paying more attention to the background and crash a hell of alot. In fact one of the biggest problems with the game is the fact that it mixes so many playstyles in together. In a single level you can go from driving a Tank to a motor bike to a Jetpack. Which is cool but does mean that you are not likely to get anything approaching a good time the first time through a lot of levels as you wrestle with the widely different handling of each mode. 

One of the other downsides to the more polished and flashy levels is the length of the game. Even with a bunch of deaths I still beat the game in about two hours. Obviously I will go back and try to ace the levels which should double the time but compared to the length of Trials Fusion this is depressingly short. 

Along with that the game never reaches the challenge of Fusion. You never really have to be that precise with your controls and it's only in the last level that you start to face more advanced challenges.

Having said all of that there is still a bunch of stuff I have yet to do in the game outside of the story missions. There are five secret keys to find each with a clue to where it is along with rewards for acing levels. 

For £11.99GBP I think it is a tight little package. If you go in expecting something on the scale of Fusion then you are going to be disappointed but by the same token there are no levels in dull static warehouses to grind through. I like Fusion but it was pretty padded in some areas and this game doesn’t do that. Choosing to cut out all of the fat to give you pure spectacle.

review:
TL;DR: Buy if you're a big fan of Trials, wait for a sale if you're not. 7.5/10. It's really fun and has some decent replay value. Eff the haters who only have 20-30 minutes on record giving negative reviews.


If you honestly believe the negative reviews of this game then you should think about who you trust. People down here dissing this game for saying it's so short and easy and they only have 20-30 minutes on record. This just shows you that they haven't even started to play this game. I am an expert trials player and i just finished the game at the 3.7 hour mark. I got 100% gold or platinum on every track in trials fusion in under 2 hours. This is a really cool game and refreshing to say the least. It is certainly not a standard trials game. I was hoping for some time trial maps and expert difficulty tracks. Since this game doesn't have that it doesn't take away from it. This game wasn't meant for that. Instead of me instantly coming to write a negative review, I looked down and noticed that i wasn't a douche bag so I decided to review the game for what it is. It is a VERY fun 3-4 hour game with moderate replay value down the road. If you don't like trials you should wait for a sale but if you like trials or fun, silly games pick this badboy up. In all seriousness as a trials fan I'd give this a solid 7.5/10. I'm not IGN so 7.5 doesn't mean its the worst game of all time. It's really cool. Pick it up!

name: Trials of the Blood Dragon
review:
This game is kinda in the middle, but I had a lot of fun playing it, so I gave it a thumbs up. 

Here is what I think about it: 
IF you are a JUST a Trials or Blood Dragon fan, wait for this to go on sale. 
IF you are a fan of BOTH Trials and Blood Dragon, then buy it. 

Only major issues I had were: 1) Too much of the "off bike" parts, as I would call it. 2) The game was too short. Took me 2-3 hours to beat the main campain. And 3) The tracks weren't hard enough towards the later parts of the game. (Not including the Extreme ones, which are secret levels)

name: Trials of the Blood Dragon
review:

The story in this game is entertaining and the soundtrack is great. The A E S T H E T I C S are on point but man the controls are just awful. I enjoyed the voice acting, and I think the game is very pretty, for what kind of game it is. However if you aren't usually a fan of the sidescrolling biker games, prepare to be frustrated. And ultimately.. While I really enjoyed the story.. For this price, I wouldn't really recomend this game to others unless you REALLY like this genre.

name: Trials of the Blood Dragon
review:
Why did Red Lynx waste so much time on this when they could have been working on a sequel to Fusion? This is not a Trials game foremost, but most of all, it's not a good game period. It bases its spirit on Trials but loses the entire point and becomes a jack of all trades for a hodgepodge of different game types haphazardly assembled together. Do you like absolutely awful, floaty side scrolling shooters with the worst enemy A.I. you've ever seen? Do you like pointless distracting additions to trials tracks like shooting targets instead of focusing on time and technique? Do you like weird RC car segments with frustrating controls that reverse on you? Do you like excessively intrusive story sequences in the style of esurance commercials and cheesy throwbacks to absurd 70's action movies? Well Blood Dragon wants to do all sorts of things, none of them any good and all cobbled together so you can't separate the ones you don't want to play. 

It's just not a good game and we could have been that much closer to a proper Trials sequel if they didn't get on this weird DLC bandwagon for introducing ridiculous internet humor inspired remixes like riding a unicorn. They lost focus on the core of Trials, hopefully they turn it around.

They nailed the feel of the decade they were aping but it never felt like it led to anything except for annoying audioclips replaying when you restart.

I went into this game with high hopes, and the first ten minutes I had a huge smile on my face, but it fails to stick the landing so comprehensivly that my only recommendation is don't buy this game, because its trash, and super sort trash at that, hit then ending at 2 1/2 horu and the style of game means that it has zero replayability trying to get things perfect like a regular trials game does

Within my 3.3 hours current gametime I've completed the main story as well as the extra tracks from the ubisoft rewards points and the secret ones. (Which involves finding secrets in game) £12 is reasonable, But the length feels like it wasn't worth that exact amount. If the game goes on sale eventually for a bit cheaper I would definitely recommend you pick it up. The game itself was extremely fun with an interactive storyline, good voice acting and funny cutscenes. It's a heavily story driven game with great encounters and an all round great game. Just as I said, it's not a Trials clone. So don't be disappointed if you buy it expecting that.

name: Trials of the Blood Dragon
review:
Trials of the Blood Dragon is a nice departure from the Trials series. If you are lookig for a normal Trials game, then this title is not for you. However, if you are open to changes, then I highly recommend this game. There are a few tedious sections here and there, but you will be able to get over it and enjoy the game for what it is.

Like the normal series, the Trials sections get more and more difficult throughout the progression of the game. Sadly, I felt as though it was not super challenging until the very last mission. So I blew through all of the Trials missions with ease, but I still had fun because it's just like Trials! Every other mission was either 'okay' or 'good', nothing bad to come from this game, really.

I have never played Far Cry 3: Blood Dragon (FPS isn't really my cup of tea unless I'm playing multiplayer with friends), but I thought that I should probably give that a look after playing this game. The aesthetics are super nice and the music is VERY good.

If I had one complaint, it's that this game is way too short. I beat this in only two and a half hours and there's no real replay ability in it. It would just be the same missions over and over again. I thought that this game would be a lot better with a level editor or something to go with it so that people can create and share their own missions. That would make me want to play this game more and more.

In conclusion, this game is really fun. It has a fantastic soundtrack and a pretty vaporwave art style. I do not recommend getting this game for $15. If this game goes for sale for around $5 then you should definitely pick it up. It's short and full of adventure!

name: Trials of the Blood Dragon
review:
I want to say out of the gate that a I think it is very unjustified and unfair to give this game negative reviews for not being a Trials Fusion clone, additionally, these complaints are coming from people with less that 30 minutes in game. Trials of the Blood Dragon like Far Cry 3: Blood Dragon was never intended to be a successor or continuation of the parent game. If you were expecting that you should look elsewhere because you will likely be disappointed. Aside from similar gameplay mechanics Trials of the blood Dragon like Far Cry 3: Blood Dragon was supposed to be a fun departure from the main game and I believe that is exactly what they have done.

I went into this game expecting what I got from Far Cry 3: Blood Dragon, which was an absolutely ridiculous, over the top, stylized, comedic parody of 80s video games and action films, and that is exactly what I got. The game features anther excellent soundtrack from Power Glove which really compliments the exaggerated 80s style of the game. In addition to the new look Trials of the Blood Dragon adds new gameplay mechanics such as shooting from your bike and on foot segments. For some people these new additions may be a hit or a miss since it is a departure from what fans of previous trials games have come to expect. The bottom line is that if you go into this game expecting a Trials reskin you are going to be disappointed, for the price you get a lot of content and style for what you are paying for. Fans of Far Cry 3: Blood Dragon and its over the top 80s look should feel right at home with Trials of the Blood Dragon.

Pros:
Fantastic style
Interesting additions to gameplay
Great amount of content for the price
Another amazing soundtrack from Power Glove

Cons: 
Some new additions such as on foot segments can be tedious
(Subjective) A significant departure from past trials gameplay

review: I am a 45 yo father, probably one of the oldest people playing this game. I am a single father to my Son, who is 14 now. My son got this game for Christmas in 2021 from his uncle, so we installed it on his computer and he started playing. By the end of the week he had 24 hours on this game. This was horrible for me, as it was already hard for me to find ways to spend time with my son, as he is always out with his friends or just watching YouTube. So i decided to make a Steam account and get this game to see if I could maybe play alongside him. I loaded into the game, picked my character and world and started playing but I was stuck on what you where supposed to do. I asked my Son for help and he hosted a game for me to join. I loved it as it was the best time I had spent with my Son since my wife had died. This game has ever since brought me and my son closer again and now we actually spend time together outside the house together as well. This game reminded me that there's fun to be had in everything, and it has brought both me and my Son many happy memories.

review:
Bridge Constructor is a delightful blend of engineering puzzles and creativity that challenges your problem-solving skills in the most entertaining way possible. It's a game that manages to make the often daunting task of building bridges not only accessible but genuinely fun.

With its intuitive controls and a variety of materials at your disposal, the game provides a sandbox where imagination meets engineering. Designing and constructing bridges becomes an addictive endeavor as you strive to create sturdy structures capable of withstanding the laws of physics and the weight of traffic.

The gradual introduction of new mechanics keeps the gameplay fresh and engaging. From simple wooden bridges to complex suspension structures, each level presents a new challenge that will test your understanding of physics and construction principles.

Watching your vehicles traverse the bridges you've meticulously crafted is both nerve-wracking and immensely satisfying. Bridge Constructor offers a perfect balance between learning and enjoyment, making it an excellent choice for both casual players and engineering enthusiasts.

If you're looking for a game that exercises your brain while providing a fun and rewarding experience, Bridge Constructor is the gateway to a world of creative engineering challenges

name: Bridge Constructor
review:
Bridge Constructor is a gem of a game that seamlessly blends creativity with engineering prowess, delivering an addictive and enjoyable experience for both casual gamers and bridge-building enthusiasts alike.

The game's premise is simple yet brilliant: construct sturdy bridges to facilitate the safe passage of vehicles across various terrains and challenges. What starts as a seemingly straightforward task evolves into a delightful puzzle-solving adventure, where each level presents unique obstacles that demand strategic thinking and creative construction.

One of the game's standout features is its intuitive and user-friendly interface, making the process of building and testing bridges a breeze. The learning curve is expertly crafted, allowing players to grasp the basics quickly before diving into more complex and challenging scenarios. The gradual introduction of new materials and construction elements keeps the gameplay fresh and engaging throughout.

The game's physics engine deserves special commendation. It adds a layer of realism that makes every bridge collapse or success feel like a genuine achievement. Watching your carefully crafted structures withstand the stress of moving vehicles or, on occasion, succumb to the forces of nature is both thrilling and educational.

Bridge Constructor's blend of education and entertainment is commendable. It not only entertains players with its clever puzzles but also imparts a basic understanding of structural engineering principles. This educational aspect is seamlessly integrated into the gameplay, making the experience both intellectually stimulating and enjoyable.

With a plethora of levels, diverse environments, and a sandbox mode for endless creativity, Bridge Constructor offers a fantastic value for its price. The game's charming visuals and lighthearted approach add an extra layer of enjoyment, making it a title that can be enjoyed by players of all ages.

In conclusion, Bridge Constructor is a triumph in the puzzle and simulation genre. It strikes a perfect balance between challenge and fun, making it a must-play for anyone with an inclination toward construction and engineering or those seeking a uniquely satisfying gaming experience. Building bridges has never been this entertaining!

name: Bridge Constructor
review:
The game falls down pretty badly on it's simulation of "real-life". The math behind the game is flawed and over simplified. Also, you are constantly limited by money and materials, which heavily stifles any design flair you might want to express. Complex and interesting designs are out of the question, because you can’t afford them. All the time you are reduced to the most basic designs. Add to that the fact that there isn’t a analysis mode that allows you to understand the forces on your bridge, and you end up with a rather basic game, that can be somewhat limited. 
Having said that, it's still fun and certainly about the best one of this type you can get. I just wish they had a "sandbox" mode.  
So, recommended? When it's on sale, yes, it is fun, just don't expect it to be too real-life, and don't for heavens sake pay full price for it!

name: Bridge Constructor
review:
[h1]Overview:[/h1]
'[i]Bridge Constructor[/i]' is one of many construction games where the player must build a bridge sturdy enough to carry a variety of vehicles from one side to the other while keeping within budget and without the bridge breaking. You play as a bridge engineer as you build your way through 5 islands all of which are in desperate need of bridges because for some reason roads are paved but blueprints for bridges are left unaddressed. You go from building simple and straightforward bridges to gigantic monstrosities that more or less resemble roller coasters than an actual bridge. Although the idea sounds fun enough, it soon becomes more stressful and frustrating than fun and amusing and that is due to a multitude of reasons.

[h1]Gameplay:[/h1]
Honestly these types of games aren't my cup of tea so take this review with a grain of salt. It might not be as bad as I portray it to be as I myself am not a physics major but I think many agree that the physics in the game alone are inconsistent and not very good. Although it didn't take me extremely long to understand how the mechanics worked, it still didn't change the fact that the things you have to build in this game are absurdly ridiculous. The window of constructing bridges in later levels become so small and tight that it feels like there are only one or two possible solutions and that has a lot to do with the budget feature in the game. It's like every island you're contracted to had budget cuts so severe that I could've sworn I heard the board talking about implementing loot boxes to their marketing campaign to increase revenue.

I can understand giving players a challenge by limiting the resources they have to build a bridge, but the bar they set for money conservation is so high that you practically need to make the most flimsy and extremely brittle overpasses that coast on the verge of breaking just because the city said "[i][b]Hey, that's too much money for a bridge that'll keep our citizens safe! Take this instead![/b][/i]". The foundations and materials also range from wood, metal, stone pillars and wires and depending on what location and level you're playing, you're only given specific ones. So at the end of the day, you're not only limited to a strict budget, but also the resources the game provides you with. You don't get to unlock better materials, different foundations or anything that could give the player creative freedom. It's just what the game says you can use which limits potential solutions even more.

Had there been a casual mode where annoying budgets were nonexistent, I feel like the game might actually be fun. But as is, the limited resources the player's given as a whole makes the entire game a frustrating trek because the city just doesn't give a damn how dangerous a bridge is, as long as the vehicles testing it make it across. They don't care if it's next to impossible to build a bridge with a $20,000 budget, just make it happen! I at one point even had a bridge tested and break just to reset it and have it somehow hold together proving that the physics don't even follow their own rules. It becomes less about "[i][b]what awesome bridge can I build?[/b][/i]" and more about "[i][b]what the fuck actually works?[/b][/i]". It's just a game that tries to be like more popular bridge constructor games but fails to do so on more than one account.

[h1]Controls:[/h1]
Besides placing anchors on a grid, there's not much else to talk about in regards to controls as most of it is just observing the finished product.

[h1]Graphics:[/h1]
Not bad but not great either. Resembles other typical bridge constructor games and looks [i]very[/i] mobile-like. That may or may not be a bad thing depending on preference but personally I'm not a fan of them. Unlike the tanker, cars and trucks behave like toys, there's no damage or anything when they fail a bridge test. As for the trailer, that's probably the most fun because when it falls, it explodes! Nevertheless, the graphics are very underwhelming and just makes you feel like you're playing a mobile game.

[h1]Audio:[/h1]
Also not great. The sounds and music work fine enough but nothing stands out or sounds particularly great. The sound effects consist of below average vehicle sounds (some to which abruptly cut off) along with the annoying sound of bridges collapsing that you'll very soon come to hate. The music also gets repetitive and annoying fast but thank god there's a way to mute it because I need peace and quiet while I build the world's ugliest, flimsiest and most dangerous bridge with as little money as possible.

[h1]Conclusion/TL;DR:[/h1]
'[i]Bridge Constructor[/i]' is a physics-based puzzle game with a huge learning curve where you build bridges with specific materials while on a strict budget. The concept has been done more times than needed but the game itself is unfortunately one of the black sheeps of the flock. The budget feature is the main killer for many players (myself included) and cripple almost every potential (and safe) solution most levels could possibly have because the maximum amount of money you're allowed to spend is unrealistically low. This means only very few bridge options work and all fly dangerously close to succumbing which makes some levels almost impossible to beat. It also praises itself for having a cool physics engine but even the engine is inconsistent as some bridges will fail one time but pass the second. Overall, '[i]Bridge Constructor[/i]' tries to be like many of it's brothers and sisters but ended up being the ugly duckling of bridge constructors.

name: Bridge Constructor
review:
[b]TLDR[/b] A traditional bridge construction game, didn't age particularly well. [11/20⭐, 21h🏅, 4/5💡]

[b]Context[/b] Bridge Constructor is traditional bridge construction game, currently including several free DLCs that largely improve playtime. Unfortunately, it didn’t age particularly well, but at least it made me appreciate [url=https://steamcommunity.com/id/macwh/recommended/684410/]Bridge Constructor Portal[/url] much more.

[b]Writing[/b] You’re a bridge builder and have to build bridges, that’s it.

[b]Aesthetics[/b] The visuals are simple without anything really standing out. (I suffered from a bug where fog turned opaque pink, which seems to be known for a long time). The soundtrack is pretty lousy and irritating, I had to turn it off a few levels into the game.

[b]Gameplay[/b] This is as traditional as a bridge construction game can get. You have to build bridges to make vehicles reach the other side of a cliff, with different anchor points to fix the structure. There are a few different materials, with different costs, and each level has a limited budget.

Personally, I found the levels in the main game to be too repetitive (this improved in the DLCs with slopping bridges) and the budget too restrictive for creative solutions (Bridge Constructor Portal registered the budget for scores but without limiting the player).

The levels are not particularly difficult with the regular vehicles, but the heavier ones can get pretty challenging. But this is mostly because the physics are so flimsy and the controls so rough that it provides for a frustrating experience of fine-tuning. Minimal changes can have unpredictable impact, and execution is not even deterministic (subsequent tries can have different results).

The interface itself is very rudimentary. It’s not possible to select which edges are roads, you can only move a point at a time, there is no option to speed up runs, it’s difficult to delete the expected piece, clicking the menu options places pieces. (Most of these have improved in Bridge Constructor Portal).

You can always restart a level and undo. Progress is auto-saved and the state of each bridge is saved.

[b]Value[/b] It took me 21 hours to beat the ~90 levels of all free expansions with all kinds vehicles, so the price seems adequate. You can always revisit previous levels, and there are multiple slots to save solutions. There’s no support for user levels. You only unlock the tank truck after finishing the levels with other vehicles, so you need to beat all levels twice. Achievements are mostly connected to passing the levels with different transport types, with a few exceptions that are easily achievable along the game (except for “Storm Warning” that I still don’t understand how I triggered it). Replay value tied to optimizing the cost of the bridges, if you’re into that kind of thing.

[quote][i]For more puzzle-game reviews and curated lists check out the [url=https://store.steampowered.com/curator/43326007-The-Clueless-Adventurer-Puzzle-Trove/]Puzzle Trove[/url].[/i][/quote]

name: Bridge Constructor
review:
I played through the first island and there's a lot of things in this game that are frustrating for no reason or could be easily improved. 

1) There's no consistency on the availability of building materials. You unlock steel cables, but you can't use them on this level... or this one! Ha ha! Even the final bridge of the first island was wood only. Complete BS.

2) The UI is very clunky. You click on the button for wood or steel and half the time it plants a node right next to the button and you have to pan the view around to delete it. Frustrating.

3) All stress is one color, there's no visual differentiation between compression or tension. I played a bridge builder 20 years ago that had compression as blue and tension as red. The engine obviously knows which is which but the developer is just being lazy.

4) There's no drag and drop for nodes. Other bridge builders will let you drag an existing node to shorten or lengthen supports and it will display the cost difference live. This game makes you rip the node and everything connected to it out. 

5) There's no mouse over text or tool tips for any material. "Ok good job you unlocked steel, but we're not going to tell you how strong it is or how much it costs!"

6) There's levels that are very obviously designed to let you get a specific achievement. "You came in $25k under budget on a 3 section span? Nice job Financial Genius or whatever." Achievements should be created from the game that exists, not drive the design of the game.

7) Finally, you get the whole island completed to 100% and they are like, "Ok now you can have tanker trucks use your bridges!" and all of your progress drops to 83%. Nope, uninstalling forever goodbye.

Bridge Constructor is fun, and often challenging. Especially early in the game, if you don't remember anything from having built matchstick bridges and buildings in school 20 years ago. Sorry, was that suspiciously specific?

Well-designed, consistent mechanics in an enjoyable leisurely atmosphere. I definitely recommend giving this one a go.
