Ahoy, submarine enthusiasts! Silent Hunter 5®: Battle of the Atlantic is your ticket to commanding a German U-boat in the most thrilling underwater warfare experience ever crafted. Step behind the periscope and immerse yourself in the depths of the Atlantic Ocean and the Mediterranean Sea, where you'll face off against the formidable Allied Forces in some of the most iconic naval battles of World War II. 🌊🔭

The best-selling submarine series continues its legacy with a bang, offering an incredibly detailed and realistic simulation of submarine warfare. Whether you're meticulously plotting your course, managing your crew, or engaging in intense naval skirmishes, every moment in Silent Hunter 5 is a deep dive into strategic depth and immersive action. 🎯🕵️‍♂️

From the high tension of evading enemy depth charges to the satisfaction of a successful torpedo strike, this game captures the essence of submarine combat with stunning accuracy. The atmosphere is palpable, and the historical context adds layers of authenticity that make every mission feel like a true adventure under the waves. 🚢💥

In summary, Silent Hunter 5®: Battle of the Atlantic is a must-play for fans of submarine simulations and naval history alike. It’s a grand voyage into the silent depths of the ocean where your strategic prowess and command skills will be put to the ultimate test. Dive in and take command! 🌊⚓🎮

name: Silent Hunter 5: Battle of the Atlantic
review: While the entire series is pretty iconic, of all of the Silent Hunter games, I feel like this one is overall "the best" in the present day. Though SHI3 is probably the most realistic of the bunch, the graphics are very dated, and it's tough to play on high-resolution screens. SH4 improved on that, but then made the targeting (even manual) far too "arcadey", and the control scheme is still difficult to use without making tons of silly mistakes. And with 5, everything just feels easier to use. The controls don't make me do things I didn't expect to happen, the targeting (while still arcadey) is more intuitive, the UI more compact, and the game doesn't look too bad at all either. The music and sounds are really enjoyable to listen to as well. I'd say, pick this one up (maybe on sale) if you get the chance, and you're a pretty strong naval combat fan.

name: Silent Hunter 5: Battle of the Atlantic
review:
I GOT IT WORKING!

Uplay has been replaced by Ubisoft Connect. I installed Ubisoft Connect separately (download here: https://ubisoftconnect.com/en-US/) and the game launched fine (On Steam Deck, I ran the installer exe through Silent Hunter 5's prefix on Wine Protontricks). All I had to do was log in on Ubisoft Connect when the game launched and then retrieve a CD key from the "Manage" tab of the settings on the game's page in my Steam Library to register it with my Ubisoft account. Turning off "Half-rate shading" made unreadable text perfectly clear; on Steam Deck it's a toggle in the quick performance settings menu on the right side of the screen. I haven't seen any bugs as yet either. Time compression is unlocked in the tutorial by placing a waypoint on the map close enough to the naval base it asks you to dock at. I enjoyed what little I've played so far of Silent Hunter 4 and this game looks like more Silent Hunter 4 only now you can wander around the sub, which is a really nice touch for immersion in my opinion (even if going up and down the ladders is a little finicky). From the limited context I've got so far it's definitely worth the $2.50 I paid for the fun I'm having as a casual sim enthusiast.

EDIT:
Okay, time for a proper review. There are some gameplay/QOL optimization issues but I personally feel from my experience with Silent Hunter 4 that this is the best game in the Silent Hunter series. I didn't realize how much I wanted the freedom (and necessity) to move about the boat freely in first-person. I booted up 3 and 4 for comparison and the toolbar system with fixed station views, while more immediately accessible to new players, quickly becomes restricting to immersion; you're constantly reminded that you're playing a game. In Silent Hunter 5, you can't dive if you're on deck nor open the top hatch unless you're on the surface; you have to physically go below deck to dive and then you're stuck in there until you surface. Depending on your platform and what keybindings you have available and make use of you can make it so you have to click on the periscopes to use them, go and talk to your officers to give orders to them (activating certain temporary abilities you can level up), and (when you're surfaced) climb up to the deck to use the sighting equipment or the guns.

Also, the campaigns can take a long, long time (especially with infinite fuel and power turned off, so that you have to keep an eye on your reserves and head back to port as needed to resupply). All in all, despite putting the extremely helpful gauges for fuel and power that were part of the HUD in SH4 behind a keybinding, this is the most complete simulator of being a WWII submarine captain I've yet encountered. If you're still on the fence about buying this and enjoy Silent Hunter or submarine simulator games I highly encourage you to get this while it's on sale. $2.50 is a steal for a game this complete and enjoyable (albeit flawed in the things it doesn't tell you; I recommend looking up the manual online, it's one of those old games that needed one). I liked Silent Hunter 4 because I enjoy WWII American submarines because I'm a classic movie fan, but I personally am enjoying this game much more even though I'm in a U-boat because I actually feel like a submarine captain (and I am deeply enjoying not having to endure the creepy stare of the XO in Silent Hunter 4). I also highly recommend this for Steam Deck as it runs perfectly on there once you install Ubisoft Connect and get the game registered as detailed above.

name: Silent Hunter 5: Battle of the Atlantic
review:
Silent Hunter 5: Battle of the Atlantic is a game where I spent many hours honing my skills in submarine warfare.  Ubisoft Connect is required to play, so make sure you have the latest version installed.  The game launched with many bugs and issues, so head over to the subsim website for lots of information on patches and mods that can be installed to fix those problems and improve the game no end.

It captures the vibes of Das Boot perfectly, giving you the opportunity to command your own U-Boat in the waters of the Atlantic, attacking conveys of shipping while avoiding destroyers and aerial attacks.  Planning an attack can be a time-consuming and stressful exercise: Tracking your targets with the periscope, positioning your sub and choosing precisely the right moment to fire your precious torpedoes.

For added realism, you can programme the Torpedo Data Computer (TDC) yourself using target speed, Angle on the Bow (AOB), torpedo running depth and impact triggers.  There's plenty of information on this over at the subsim website as well as tutorial videos on YouTube.  

Working this all out with a notepad and protractor next to the computer is an experience worth trying, as a successful hit feels even more sweet given all the effort you put in!  You also get a small sense of what it must have been like for the submariners who did this in real life, and the the dangers they faced.

name: Silent Hunter 5: Battle of the Atlantic
review:
I'm usually very relaxed when it comes to game reviews, however I am unimpressed with Silent Hunter V. The game is plagued with bugs (something you should expect from Ubisoft), and often requires you to jump through additional hoops to even get the thing to launch when you want to play.

Playing the game is slow, but if you have patience and bugs don't glitch you out it is pretty fun. I've spent hours on the game only for a bug to prevent me from entirely rearming (You have to travel from where you're at in game back to home for this in some circumstances which is anywhere from 5-15 minutes on fastest forward speed- if you don't hit anything). I've essentially game equivalent fast-traveled back to the sub-pens only to discover I'm under the map and abandoning the mission ruins all progress, etc. If the game is on sale and you don't have anything else to do, I'd encourage you to get it. If not, I'd say keep your money for something like War Thunder submarine and ship play. 

I just wish the game was better developed and ran more smoothly, otherwise I'd give it glowing reviews. Buy at your own risk, either you need to mod it to hell for it to be more than a shell or you're truly bored out of your mind. One of the prominent reasons behind my negative review is I frown upon game developers who rely on the modding community to keep their game bug free and entertaining. If it wasn't for some dedicated people, the game would likely be history and unable for play aside from emulation. But as I mentioned prior, what else would you expect from Ubisoft? A quality product in 2023? Nah.

name: Silent Hunter 5: Battle of the Atlantic
review: So Far the Steam Version and TWOS mod have been very stable on Windows 10 Home.  I have had zero issues with Ubisoft Connect interfering.  I believe Steam has disconnected the game from that service.  As far as Submarine warfare simulations go, this the best one I have found up and running today.  Keep in mind, it is a Captain Simulator not a Sub Simulator.  Play it like a FPS with your weapon being the crew and the submarine.  It takes lots of patience and some effort to learn how the systems work and how to use your weapons.  Learn to use the Range and Angle on Bow Finder, Attack Disk, and set up the Torpedo Data Computer.  Computer is a loose term in 1939 and it is more like a mechanical slide rule.  It is worth it to purchase the "U-Boat Commanders Handbook" (U.Kdt.Hdb.).  It is a reprint of the actual lesson's learned document issued to real U-Boat Commanders.  It has a lot of good advice and much of what you see in the movies is just plain wrong.  For example, poor periscope discipline will get you killed quickly in a Convoy battle.  Learn the game, stick to it, and it is very rewarding to survive a Convoy fight and even more so when you sink a ship or two.  Youtube has a lot of tutorials on it and go to Subsim Forums.  Help is out there.  Great game that is not for the instant gratification, short attention span crowd.

name: Silent Hunter 5: Battle of the Atlantic
review:
I would recommend this game as your entry point into Silent Hunter series if you are new and do not know how to program a torpedo solution on your own. This game will make most of the calculations for you and  even allow you to optimally strike targets with WILD gyro angles on them.  All you have to do is view the target in your periscope and press SPACE to lock it in.  The  crew will calculate the shot for you, no need to meddle with targeting or any tools.

That being said, if you want the TRUE silent hunter series experience with this game, there is a mod that is called Wolves of Steel that you can install.  You can find it by googling or check  the Subsim website.  That mod will essentially turn this game into SH3 Remastered.  SH3 imo was the best game in the series followed by the 4th game, because it was aimed towards simulation enthusiasts that enjoyed using plotting tools, stop watches, attack-disc or RAOB disks, and doing basic trigenometry to aquire accurate firing solutions for torpedos - that realism imo was made those games fun and immersive.

SH5 out of the box experience does not have any of that as they made this game as accessible as possible by removing those tools and the need for them.  This is why I said this might be a good entry level point for you - and when you are ready for a more true WW2 uboat sim, go grab the Wolves of Steel mod.

Otherwise if modding isn't for you, I would recommend you go with SH4 or SH3.

name: Silent Hunter 5: Battle of the Atlantic
review:
In Silent Hunter 5 you're back in the North Atlantic ocean
as a U-boater.  
So basically the settings for Silent Hunter 3, but with better
graphics and widescreen resolutions out of the box.

You can wander around your submarine now, it's an interactive 3D space

This can get annoying though, if you're out on deck when an enemy 
warship suddenly shows up, you have to physically run back and scale
the conning tower and down a ladder via hatch before you can order a dive. 
Unfortunately by then your sub might be peppered with shells and 
sinking.
(Although Shift+U will teleport you from the deck
to the top of the conning tower so all you have to do is jump into the
hatch and order a submerging dive)

Also, there is at least one serious bug I've encountered once or twice which can be game
breaking - the sunk tonnage meter on the campaign missions got stuck  

This game copped a lot of negative reviews, and I think it's primarily
because a lot of people have trouble getting this game started initially.

That's fair criticism, because Ubisoft should have updated the Steam
version to run Ubisoft Connect out of the box (It's still running
some ancient copy of Uplay! No wonder it doesn't boot...)

The secret is to have an up-to-date version of Ubisoft Connect installed prior to running this game from Steam.
Here's a useful guide on how to do that:-
https://steamcommunity.com/app/48110/discussions/0/3278068255907253397/

name: Silent Hunter 5: Battle of the Atlantic
review:
WARNING: THIS GAME ONLY WORKS IF YOU DO THE FOLLOWING
1) After downloading the game and BEFORE installation you HAVE to download Ubisoft Connect, create an account and enter the game's activation key. Otherwise when you go to install the game you'll run into a Ubisoft network error. 

2) Install the game, then close it.

3) Go to the Subsim website and download the Wolves of Steel super mod and self installer. Make sure the mod is installed in the same directory as Silent Hunter 5.

4) Start the game. If you start it and it shows up in a window in the corner of your screen with no audio and response; then just let the video play all the way till the end and then close steam, if that doesn't remove the window then restart your computer.

5) Go to the Silent Hunter .exe location, usually found in c:/steam/steamapps/common/Silent Hunter 5. 
Right-click  SH5.exe, select Properties, select the Compatibility tab, and then select the Disable display scaling on high DPI settings check box.

6) Run the game and enjoy.

AGAIN, this game is completely unplayable without all these steps so avoid it completely if you are unwilling or unable to do all of them. Ubisoft released a broken game and never patched or fixed it properly but thankfully the diligent and talented folks over at Subsim created a brilliant megamod that not only makes the game work for once but also creates a rich, immersive experience that's unequaled in other sub simulators.

name: Silent Hunter 5: Battle of the Atlantic
review:
Buy Silent Hunter Wolves of the Pacific.
In SH5, you are the  first-person  captain and move around your sub. You have to do everything by the letter. There is no shortcut. For example, if you need to dive or crash dive, and you're on the deck, well.. you need to move first, go down the stairs, then you can dive. You can't change direction when backing up. There is no easy way to change the bearing without tracing it on the map. Defining the depth is a challenge as the control is very small. Shooting torpedoes is also a challenge, even in automatic mode. Need to check with the Observation then Attack periscopes? That's easy.. run dand climb the stairs to switch. Add other bugs in the game with the mouse. Time compressing sucks. For example, if you're travelling long range and encounter a DD, time-compressing will be set to 32, still too fast and all you can see is the damage done to the sub, or miss completely a convoy. Exiting time-compressing should have been reset to 1.
Sure, Silent Hunter Wolves of the Pacific is buggy.. But at least, it does not have all of these non-sense.

name: Silent Hunter 5: Battle of the Atlantic
review:
I've played this game for 250.6 hours, and here are the issues I've run into. Tonnage, and number of ships sunk is not recorded in the missions in the Campaign.cfg file, despite the ships sunk is recorded in the CampaignProgess.cfg file. I have resorted to recording the ships sunk and tonnage in a separate worksheet, like U-boat captains in the old days. I then go into Campaign.cfg, after I save and exit, then update the tonnage and number of ships sunk, and change “false” to “true” for each mission completed in the campaign. I am forced to use the silentotto command to move from one campaign to the next. Another problem is diving. If I go beyond 50 meters, the ship keeps diving, until I blow ballast, and reset the depth above where I am rising from.  For this reason I have to run the game with unlimited compressed air. 

Ubisoft should be ashamed for putting this game out with so many problems, and not issuing a patch for it. Their quality as a game company has dropped sharply since the days I played Rogue Spear and Splinter Cell. Glad I only paid $10 for it. I think with these issues even that price is too much. Save your money, and play Silent Hunter 4 instead, or play something new.

name: Silent Hunter 5: Battle of the Atlantic
review:
This is a great  ww2 submarine marred by lack of publisher support and drm issues.
I recommend this based on the game, but with a warning that many experience issues getting it working (I didn't).

Be prepared to spend hours learning the most basic aspects as you get to grips with one of the best subsims ever created.
Yes it's old and buggy, and needs some essential mods (wolves of Steel) but it's a great game.
You control a sub and must manage almost every aspect. Careful planning of your movements to get yourself into conveys undetected, then launching your attack before quickly taking evasive actions. Fantasticly tense action interspersed with periods of more mundane navigation.
Thank god you never had to go through this for real in ww2. Silent hunter 5 is as real as it gets. Fantastic (if it works)

name: Silent Hunter 5: Battle of the Atlantic
review:
So, Just to preface my positive review, [b]you MUST get the Wolves of Steel mega-modset for this game off of the Subsim(dot)com for it to work right.[/b] A quick google search will direct you to the forum to find it.

With the modset, the game is amazing. Manually inputting info into the TDC, customizing the game-play to the level of realism you want (within reason), and sinking tonnage. There is nothing more satisfying than sneaking a volley of torpedoes through a destroyer screen to sink a Queen Elizabeth-class Battleship, and get away with it, or hit a 7km torpedo shot because you did the proper calculations and they never even knew you were there. (personal experiences, yours may vary) There are still a few bugs here and there in the modset, but once you recognize them, a quick save and reload will fix them up. 
In the Wolves of Steel modset, there are historical events added in via received radio messages, which actually happened during the war, such as Operation Wikinger, (you can look it up) or during Operation Weserübung, which is an in-game mission, but had a lot happen during the operation to take over Norway, while you are tasked to sink tonnage in the area.

Without the mod-set, well... the missions don't really work, you can still manually fire the torpedoes and get the same shots described above to hit with the manual TDC input, but there is no guarantee it will count for your objective in the area. There are "tonnage bar" quotas to certain missions, but rather than a certain class of tonnage to be sunk, like "merchant tonnage," the game uses certain ship files for what needs to be sunk,("Merchant ship_124" or something like that) which are only spawned in specific groups, so you could sink 2 large freighters for 7k tonnes each, for your 20k tonne quota, but neither will count because they weren't the right ship for the game to recognize, or weren't flying the right flag.

Ubisoft pushed this game out in a complete mess, like a lot of their other games from the time period that are notorious for being buggy, because at the time, they realized that Assassin's Creed was what was making them money. They dropped everything they couldn't fit to their new AC formula, which is easily recognizable as, "Find tall tower, reveal area, activities and collectibles, do activities and pickup collectibles." Said formula was successful enough that other companies copied it. *cough cough Horizon*( I loved HZD btw, story was awesome, no hate)
After that, it was up to modders to support and fix. And they really did. With mods from Subsim, this game is still pretty relevant. Without them, you are better of buying one of the other U-boat games out there. But all mods are free, unless you wish to donate to the modders, if they are still doing that, that is.

That being said, i would recommend at least starting a game without the WoS mod just so you can experience the beginning storyline of being promoted from First Officer to Captain of the boat. Otherwise if you don't care about that, start with the mod and make up your own story(in your head) about how you were given command of the boat.

$10 for this game is pushing it for its released version, so if you can get it cheaper, that would be better. This game is sadly one that REQUIRES mods to get your moneys worth out of and actually get to work right.

I have no experience with SH3 or SH4, so this is truly a review based on this game and genre. I have heard both are better than this in terms of gameplay, but are limited in the fact that you can not walk around the sub. Rather, you are restricted to switching between stations. 

Side note, it does require having a Uplay account and it seems people are having difficulty with updates due to dropped support. I can't hold the same claim, but i also have had the game for a very long time, so take that as you will.


Good hunting, Herr Kaleun. o7

name: Silent Hunter 5: Battle of the Atlantic
review:
2021 review for the "Wolves of Steel" modded version of the game: Soon after installation I was googling  "how do ships navigate at sea?" "How do I use a sextant?", "how did the ENIGMA machine work?" Yes, with mods this game includes an option to decode encrypted messages using a working Enigma machine and the option to navigate manually using a working sextant and other navigational tools. Basically, if you get this game, you have to download the amazing WOS modpack which comes with its own installer for steam and very specific instructions that are easy to follow. Be warned as the mod can make certain aspects *very* realistic - even playing other sub sims did not prepare me for some of the available options. 

Thankfully, the difficulty can be adjusted by turning on/off certain optional mods that come with the Wolves of Steel mod set so you dont have to learn everything at once. The modpack adds a tonnnnnnnnn of features and it makes me feel like I am playing an MMO - it really does feel like there's SO MUCH going on and you are only one small part of a much larger war. Example; first attempt at sinking an enemy destroyer was aided by the fact it had been damaged by a squadron of friendly divebombers I saw returning to base (thanks for the help little guys!). Docks are massive facilities inside of living port cities with ships coming and going, the occasional recon plane flying overhead... very immersive game. Despite its age, the mod makes this game look pretty incredible at times - the way light interacts with smoke and mist during night attacks? Havent seen another game do that as well. It's quite pleasant to hang out on the conning tower and watch the waves. 

Given the years of work that went into the mod, the game is well worth the money if you are looking to get into sub sims. Very satisfying game to learn and think about; it's a great feeling when everything starts to make sense.

name: Silent Hunter 5: Battle of the Atlantic
review:
Great game ,Best open water sim there is , Biggest open world map in gaming .Sky is correct for any date , moon phases and constellations,Volumetric clouds and game engine deliver weather patterns , beautiful sunsets .Good looking game even 10 years later .
It can be( was for me ) a pain to get started . You need Wolves of steel Mod to get the most from it but its entirely playable unmodded.( i would recommend playing it unmodded first . 
   Modding the game is so easy and streamlined now as to what it used to be , So anyone who is going in now gets the advantage of years spent in the pursuit of a more perfect game . 
So why all the bad press ? 
The developers implicitly said in interviews that the game was to be a canvas , to be filled in by the modding community , 
  It was left , "unfinished" by design (This is a simplification ,Obviously there were many things at play as in also it was arguably released a bit early hardware wise , This game takes a good machine even 11 years later to run flawlessly .
  Which leads to a release to a public who aren't equipped to enjoy the game for one reason or the other and they basically killed the franchise with bad reviews . 
And , I have to admit . They had a point . Even today if you buy this on steam you will have to dl uconnect or whatever to dl the game to get it working , and its a lot of hoops you shouldnt have to deal with .
But .. when this game is working correctly its immersive and its laid back . You begin to think of the sub as a thing besides just a cursor . .Its on my list of essential PC games . But .. its like that weird singer you like , Not everyone is going to "Get It "...
I recommend it but its not for everyone .

name: Silent Hunter 5: Battle of the Atlantic
review:
I love sub games and I've played all the Silent Hunters. I think 3 made the most sense. 4 was ruined by the constant air attacks. There always seems to be something unfinished about Ubisoft sub games. Silent Hunter 5 is no exception and makes you wonder why they can't seem to get it right after all the practice they've had making sub games.     
Pros: It's obvious that a great deal of creative energy and artistic talent went into making this game look stunning. Great graphics! Sea and weather effects are outstanding. Ships rendered extraordinarily well. Explosions, too. Well researched data, technologies, and history. My only tiny complaint with the graphics is with that too constant spray effect at the front of the boat.
Cons: I wish the same thought would have gone into the gameplay, which is terribly frustrating, sometimes stupid. You can't issue orders from the bridge. If you want to go to silent running you need to search for your Lieutenant down below and ask him to give the order. You can't even steer the boat from the bridge. The rudder control just gives you the option of five degrees to port" (or starboard) with the rudder amidships button unworkable, so if you are about to collide with a ship blocking the harbor entrance you have to jump to the map and reset your waypoints. When you tell your number 2 on the bridge to "Man the Flak guns" they get manned instantly but then those guys just stand there when the planes come and won't shoot. You need to man one of the guns yourself or else locate the plane with your binoculars and then hit spacebar when a little red x shows up...almost impossible to do with a fast moving plane. Mission orders keep repeating the same blather during each campaign phase. Mission objectives are confusing and often seem ludicrous...sometimes you have to look for that one Liberty Cargo ship in a 20 ship convoy to complete a mission. Really? With so many other more valuable targets in front of you? And when you DO find and sink that one ship that allows you to complete a mission objective there is a good chance that a glitch will keep it from being counted toward mission completion so you have to start that mission over again...it might be counted the next time, or not. And make sure you don't stop and attack a convoy until you dally in a certain area or all the ships you sink won't be counted either and all your torpedoes will be wasted. I never was able to complete all the mission chapters because of this glitch that didn't count my sinkings, no matter how many times i saved and started over. GOD, why can't Ubisoft at least let you progress to the other areas? Another frustration is that you are told to search a certain area and then are shown that the convoy you are supposed to be looking for is hundreds of miles away, heading away from you. The enemy AI is completely incompetent. Enemy planes show up often but they never attack. You will rarely be attacked by the protective destroyer escorts unless you are caught on the surface and then their gunnery is extremely accurate. They rarely use their sonar. You can get into the middle of any convoy with ease and do all the killing you want before they finally show up. Then all you have to do is go down and come up behind them and continue your killing spree. I have yet to be damaged by the even more rare depth charge. While the graphics are stupendous a little hand wringing with destroyers relentlessly hunting me down would have been a nice addition to the game (and a main reason for playing a sub game in the first place), something my first sub game had plenty of...Aces of the Deep. In reality sinking ships and sending men to their deaths in a frigid ocean is the height of evil cruelty but doing it in a game is addictive. Even so you'll soon tire of seeing another Liberty Cargo ship blow up and sink after doing all the steps necessary because it is so easy and there is little or no offensive action to stop you. BORING.   
You will hear that there is a mod out there called Wolves of Steel that fixes the game and makes it more realistic, but after jumping through 2001 hoops i finally got it installed and then nothing happened. Apparently the mod has a hard time dealing with the Steam version even tho the download process asks you to choose the Steam version. Yeah i did all the things subsim.com and Steam forums tells you to do to get the damn thing running, but nothing worked for me. So that wasted about a week of my life and made me angry with the mod people too for making it so complicated.Why can't somebody make a mod that you can just download and it works? 
So all that graphic work has been wasted by totally incompetent gameplay, which really makes me mad because this game could have been a really primo product...and still could be if somebody at Ubisoft cared and fixed it. What a waste!
Don't subject yourself to this beautiful mess.

name: Silent Hunter 5: Battle of the Atlantic
review:
TL:DR from a 15 year Silent Hunter 3 veteran: 
-Skipped vanilla and went straight for The Wolves Of Steel mod.
Game might be up your alley if you like to fiddle around and get things exactly how you want them the hard way but it's a fight and like me, you might not have the patience to spend time fixing Ubisofts mistakes.
I'm going back to Silent Hunter 3.

-Game has improvements over SH3 like graphics and crew interactions but many things have been made worse like the UI and all the keyboard shortcuts however to set controls to SH3 ones you need a mod which if you like WASD movement still isn't good enough since it uses the arrow keys.
-Most issues can be fixed with mods but many mods are very complex like NewUIs with TDC for SH5 by TheDarkWraith version 7.4.2 , I'm well able to fiddle around with this stuff and get it to a point where I'd be happy but I don't have the patience to mess around with that when I just want an idiot proof slap it together and boom it works solution.
-Crew animations are somewhat janky like for example, always climbing ladders at a 90 degree angle from what looks normal.(BIG sin considering Ubisoft had amazing animations in 2 Assassins Creed games prior to SH5's release.)

Suffice it to say there's enough issues that I'm going back to the tried and true Silent Hunter 3.  I've never played Silent Hunter 4 but Americans measuring depth in feet instead of proper meters like everyone else is a no from me here in Ireland.

name: Silent Hunter 5: Battle of the Atlantic
review:
I was looking forward to a nicer experience than SH3. Installed this via Steam. Then the game would not start. Some uplay popped up. Ok, download uplay from Ubisoft. Then I had to register with Ubisoft, oh well. Started the game. Cannot pick my Nic as "Robert Schwarzenberg" is too long. Ok, shorten it. Start. I am supposed to go up the tower. So I try and try and no, wont move up. Checking support and "one has to look right into the ladder and move forward". Very intuitive, sure, a similar move like smashing your own teeth on steel. Doing the Intro and all good.
Then the mission picked, british coastal waters, sank about 60000 tons after getting more and more frustrated with the crappy Torpedos, one needs quite a few to sink these Uber-Merchant vessels. Alternativly use one, come up and then use the gun, how intuitive. And yes, this is a good move close to the british coast. 
But look and behold, my mission bar does not progress. Considering that the game only offers Type VII Boats, this was the final straw.
Never before have I seen such an unintuitive UI and such an abyssmal rework of a good old game, considering this is supposed to be a follow up to Silent Hunter 3.

name: Silent Hunter 5: Battle of the Atlantic
review:
The game itself was promising, but budget cuts and a rushed development delivered an unfinished product, ridled with bugs. Thankfully, the Silent Hunter franchise has a long standing community, that transformed the game to something closer to what it should've been. I've played it very little without mods, and would advise against it, the vanilla game is playable but not to the same extent of enjoyability provided by mods. I personally use TWOS, sprinkled with a few other mods, and get a solid experience with an occasional bug here and there. This game is old, and it shows, but honestly i still think the graphics hold up. Don't buy expecting some super fancy simulator, nor a super laid back arcady "kill em all" style game.
If you are aware of the problems and familliar with modding, i'd recommend picking it up especially when on sale, as long as you're willing to go up the learning curve, especially if you're totally new to the genre. Regarding the ubisoft problems, i've been playing with no issues, but i use a modded version, so that might be the reason, since steam doesn't even log my gameplay hours (wayy more than 3).

review: I was really tough in my last comments. That's was based on being unable to download it! I found how and it installed perfectly. Playing the sim was the usual Ubisoft: full of promise......and bugs! I played the base software for about 8 hours and decided to install the megamod "The Wolves of Steel".  Playing in the TWoS universe drawn me in. Visuals in the external and internal are beautiful! There's a crew that is interactive to an extent. and 'quasi-lifelike'. They're better looking than the crash dummies that you see in other games. There's an extensive collection of navigation tools as an excellent TDC and many new features. If you're into sub/naval sims, Silent Hunter V is something you'll enjoy!  Caveat: you'll need a megamod to really get value and enjoyment.

name: Silent Hunter 5: Battle of the Atlantic
review:
Don't let all the negative reviews pull you down on this game. It's far from a bad game especially with mods, which there are plenty of them on SUBSIM. Sure, it doesn't quite live up to the standard that Silent Hunter 3 set up, especially with the GWX supermod. 

I truly recommend that if you're interested in Silent Hunter 5, go ahead and get it, then install the Wolves of Steel mod, becomes just as fun as SH3 and fixes a lot of issue that the vanilla game had. You can combine a select few mods with your Wolves of Steel to create a truly amazing experience.

With the right mods, you get a subsim with amazing gameplay and for a 2010 game, it has amazing graphics. with 3d clouds and some of the best water effects I've seen in any game to date.

Overall, if you're getting into subsims, pick up SH5 with Wolves of Steel and SH3 with GWX, both amazing contenders in the subsim genre for just 20 bucks between the two, even cheaper if you bag them on sale.

After everything is done and installed, the only issues I have with SH5 is that Wolves of steel makes it strangely laggy, vanilla game had perfect performance with 60fps, but after WoS, performance decreased substantially.... still sorting it out as many people have their own workarounds to this since SH5 is still a 32bit application, and the other issue? NO TYPE IX! You command variants of the Type VII in SH5, from the VIIA to the VIIC/41. Unlike SH3, you won't be able to eventually play with a Type IX or XXI, which makes me a very sad Kaleun.

name: Silent Hunter 5: Battle of the Atlantic
review:
I have not played long, but this game is unbearable. 
When I purchased it, I was aware of issues and prepared to fix the bug with uplay that many users prevented to even play the game. So I made the fix suggested and started playing. I played SH3 and people are claiming SH5 is more casual friendly. It is on the contrahery. I am a casual player and I am struggling even on low difficulty, because of endless bugs. On my first mission I was searching for a convoy and speed up the game. Suddenly I get damage messages and the post credits come that I was sunk. It was that moment I saw why. On a clear day, being surfaced with full moral I rammed right into an escort. Penetrated the escort right through the middle. Only the moment I saw that, the crew reported the sighting of it. Perception check failed totally. Fun fact the guns of the escort (British) were aiming away from me. How the hell was that even possible? I never had bugs like this in SH3. Perfectly aimed torpedos doing strange spreads, even though I did not put in the commands for that. The graphics are good, but the gameplay is more than horribly. There are game-breaking and I can only recommend to not spend any money on this game from Buggysoft. Since I am above the hours eligible for refund, I will try to find mods to fix the massive lacking options to properly control the game and may try to play it after that.

name: Silent Hunter 5: Battle of the Atlantic
review: Overall a great look at WWII submarine warfare from the German perspective. You can set the playstyle however for rookie players like myself it is a lot to wrap around. Quite the realistic game, so much so that my heart races every time i jump in.  I would recommend investing in a good computer to enjoy the best it has to offer.

name: Silent Hunter 5: Battle of the Atlantic
review:
I get sucked into playing this game every few years, once I start getting nostalgic for playing Silent Service on the Amiga. Sadly, the bugs that were there at launch are still there now and the more I play, the more I come across.

The submarine, the star of the show, comes so close to feeling alive and then falls short. The crew just keep repeating dialogue and switching to "battle stations" makes no discernible difference. It just feels like the entire game was a project that got to 90% finished and then they got bored and published it.

name: Silent Hunter 5: Battle of the Atlantic
review:
Very disappointed with Steam but the game is good after you fix it yourself.
Something to be aware of is this game in most cases WILL NOT PLAY after download from Steam without jumping through some hoops.  You will most likely have to fix the problems with this game yourself which is unsatisfactory but guess there's no way around that.  When I first purchased from Steam and tried to play I got the notorious "service not available" error and the game does not launch.  It appears that "Uplay" is the problem. at least it was for me.  To fix this problem I went to Ubisoft and downloaded the "OFFICIAL" updated version of Uplay.  I DO NOT RECOMMEND A 3RD PARTY site download but that's up to you.  Here is the legit Ubisoft link for the Uplay update to get Silent Hunter V to work:
https://uplay.ubisoft.com/en-GB.  After downloading and installing Uplay directly from Ubisoft the game works like it should on my 64-bit Windows 10 Pro computer with the latest windows updates.

This is something that Steam should know and take care of before taking your money and expecting you to figure things out on your own.  That is very unprofessional of Steam and makes them look very bad.  This is a widely experienced problem with Silent Service V and Steam should be all over this fix but they are not.
This worked for me but your mileage may vary.

Does any of that sound like too much work? well, rest assured, you can always go with an easier setting and avoid all that.

name: Silent Hunter 5: Battle of the Atlantic
review:
In my humble opinion,

Great series. 

I have played Wolfpack (the original from like, the 90's), Sub Command, Janes Fleet Command, Silent Hunter II, Silent Hunter III, Silent Hunter IV,  Silent Hunter V, Command Modern Air Naval Operations and Command Modern Operations. 

This game is the culmination of decades of submarine gaming. Its beautiful, but very far from perfect. 

I can say for myself, the most appealing part of the game is the ability to walk freely while aboard the sub. That adds a level of realism not seen in earlier sub games, at least to my knowledge. 

I do wish SH5 was more in-depth and open world, with a wider selection of submarines types, multiple playable sides, and perhaps a SH6 with the Pacific campaign. 

Also would love to see what a cold war version and modern day version of Silent Hunter would look like. 

Its buggy and needs a PC with some guts, but I can run it on the boat anchor I've got and still have fun. 

There is a huge following of these and other naval PC games on www.subsim.com, if you havent visited their website, I'd highly recommend a look. (I don't work for them or get paid by them or anything like that at all, just a cool site of similar topics). 

With these games I spend countless hours playing and I'm always wishing for more.

name: Silent Hunter 5: Battle of the Atlantic
review:
It is kind of a yes and a no from me. It is mostly an ok game, but compared to it's predecessors, it is obviously lacking. 

Silent Hunter 5 is beaten easily by 4th and especially the legendary 3rd game of the series when it games to gameplay and features. 

SH5 is plagued with bugs. In my playing time I have seen ships disappear in front of me, the holes from my torpedo hits disappear etc etc. UI items seem to regularly disappear or not work (I have the oxygen bug out the most), characters don't do what they are supposed to, this mainly applies to the watch officer who very often stays on the conning tower when you sink.

The looks of this game are great compared to 3 & 4, but that is to expected from a newer title. 

More merchant ship types should have been added. You are mainly sinking 3-5 types of merchant ships which makes identification way too easy and is just plain lazy by the devs. There are 2 freighters and 2 tankers. Just... lazy. There should be a lot more variety on merchant ships in this game, since the U-Boats sunk vessels built between the years 1900-1945. 

What is absolutely love about this game is the free first-person movement inside the submarine, it is a lot better than the system from previous games. BUT! This does NOT really justify having only Type VII U-Boats to available for the player. You could sail with the type VIIs, IXs and even with the Type XXI. Again, just plain lazy. 

The crew management has been simplified radically, personally I preferred the system from SH3 but I am not really against the "rpg" elements that have been tried to implement here, but again they seem kinda lazy and they do not have a lot of dept to them. I also miss earning medals and promotions to your crew, it was a nice part of SH3, I know you can do that in SH5 as well but again, it is just simplified extremely. 

The AI is also not much better than it was in earlier titles. 

Many of these issues can be fixed (sort of) with mods, but to such an extent that the mods feel essential to the game. Many of the mods I added were menus and commands and gameplay thingies that I feel like should be in the game in the first place.

Anyway, SH5 beats it's predecessors in looks alone, in almost everything else, SH3 and SH4 blow it out of the water. Compared to other sims however it is still an ok game though but In my personal opinion, a heavily modded SH3 or SH4 are way better game than any version of SH5.

name: Silent Hunter 5: Battle of the Atlantic
review:
SH III kept crashing for me, so I moved on. SH5 is quite different. Graphically it is a huge improvement as well as in other areas, like being abled to walk around the sub. But it is not all better - I miss the superb sound design for example. 

Just downloading from Steam wont do it! You need to at least download the newest Ubisoft Launcher in order for it to run. 
I can highly recommend The Wolfs of Steel Mod, as it fixes a lot of bugs and glitches next to major gameplay improvements. The installation is as simple as it can get without the Steam Workshop. It also bypasses the Ubisoft Launcher for good (but you need to run with it once befor).
Check here: https://www.subsim.com/radioroom/showthread.php?t=211804
and here: https://www.subsim.com/radioroom/showthread.php?t=210703

Fell deeply in love with the modded version. From decrypting Enigma messanges to manually calculating own position and firing solution. Realistic, hardcore, immersive. 

Gute Jagd, ihr Himmelhunde!

name: Silent Hunter 5: Battle of the Atlantic
review:
This game gets so much hate. Download Wolves of Steel, and enjoy, without this megamod I get why some SH3 diehards disliked it. I transitioned myself, as the graphics and overall experince of SH3 just was starting to seem dated. With this installed its like a brand new game, I still play it to this day simply because no one else can get a subsim right. The graphics and overall experience are both better than SH3. Only issue I have is that the type VII is the only usable sub, I really wish they added at least the type IX for long range campaigns.

For those who have issues running this with the mod on Windows 10, there are plenty of resources our there to help. I had an issue with it crashing to desktop after loading into the game, and had it fixed within 30 mins of troubleshooting. Its not game breaking and taking some time is worth the hours of gameplay in this very immersive WWII subsim.

name: Silent Hunter 5: Battle of the Atlantic
review:
Intro
This is my first review on steam. Well we all start at some point ... :-)
Btw, English is not my mother tongue (but German), but to have a global reachout I decided not to do "German first".
I bought SH5 nearly a week ago and after about 10-12 hrs of playing I am already bored and disappointed.

First some pros:
Good atmospherte overall. The languages in speaking and subtitlles (German/English ) do not match always exactly, but its good enough for me. The U-Boat performs as I was used to it in SH3 and the different settings allow for pro players (doing the math) and amateur Captains as well (simply aim the target, give some orders). 
A good feature was done by assigning points to the crew. Guess that pays off in later missions.

Now some cons:
There is an intro but overall you are guessing what to do. Not the navigation or attack, but the standard navy procedures.
When to use the radio? What orders shall be given in which order to do it properly?
As I did not serve the navy (doing my time in the army) I am not familiar with that but basic military knowledge tells me that there are some procedures for every step you do :-)
The missions are quite strange. (I admit I stopped early, so maybe I miss something here). You are ordered to sink specific tonnage or ships but even if you do so in many cases I wondered why it did not count. 
I ended up ignoring the mission orders and went on hunting as I seemed appropriate.
Also strange that you need to walk to each station to give orders yourself. I expected that you should be able to give orders more easy.
And finally - although most ships do show the battle scars after being hit by torpedo or gunfire, the big ones dont (transporters, carriers), or I simply have a bad hardware.

Overall I would not recommend to buy that game now. Maybe some bugs might get fixed in the short term, but as long as mission controls and order controls are not clear this is justr giving you frustration.

name: Silent Hunter 5: Battle of the Atlantic
review:
Wow I'm going to have to fight hard here but I have a sincere review that reveals that actual enormous VALUE this game adds to the series (if it was introduceable enough) I will.

I will say one thing about this game. Fighting and traveling are PERFECT IN THIS GAME. They are beyond, without a doubt, the most perfect controls for not only point and click traveling with time dilation adventure, but also precision like freaking Nazi astronauts. Fighting is actually BETTER than Silent Hunter Four, and I know I'll get flak for that, but for how in depth and satisfying with how the controls are in this game, without a doubt these are the most intuitive feeling controls with a torpedo submarine is "shoot it straight and then a little right and left" is such a perfectly simple control movement with this game. They must have poured 99% of their time into that. Even if it's not perfect pistons and sheers, it still works and requires judgement and skill.

What is WEIRD is the walking, and let me go beyond that, who the fuck is this german guy waking you up in the very beginning? This guy sees your eyes are open and just keeps himself in your face for too long asking if you're awake, like the fuck man? Then he skips down the corridor and uncomfortably massages the backs of people he sees, and I'm going to be honest it was fine, but the lack of body animations the rest of the crew had, it was a pretty bad scene. Going beyond that, the interior play of the game is a cool attempt and does something new but does also fail to really add any play value and actually breaks the game's flow up a little. 

I will say now, if I could just 'edit' the flow of the game from start to finish it's actually a 9.5 huge contribution to its genre /10. It performs leaps and bounds which this game has really done for itself. If one read a novel like Operation Drumbeat: U-Boat 123, this is the perfect game to have modding capabilities, or really just altered start dates to feel the soldiery of a sailor in African Germany, and that's where I'd spend my effort if the company actually hired me. Again, great game, too old to fix (maybe), good potential on many things except scary weird start of the game.

name: Silent Hunter 5: Battle of the Atlantic
review:
Wasserbomben! Oh my god people are wrong about this game and it sucked me in like hull damage in 150m below the sea.

There's a mod called "the Wolves of Steel", requires you to patch the game, update a bunch of files, and be patient while it loads. After that, it's pretty much the greatest game ever (involving submarines) after these overhaul and improvements. 

You can walk around your ship, talk to the dudes that does the hydrophone, radar, weapons guy, navigator and ask "how's your wife" and you boost his morale. Or do it via shortcut on the bottom of the screen. Then you can set sail and sink some ships. 

You can set the realism setting to however you feel like it, whether you want to calculate the firing solutions yourself, or have AOE of the detection ranges of destroyers on the map at all times, this mod enables you the option to do so. They got all the tools you need, several different compasses, protractors, rulers, a chart for sine and cosine.

Everything makes sense logically and a lot of things are simulated in this game, like weather 
 and (sun/moon/wave/broaching/wake/radio/subterfuge/sound/heat/thermals/sonar/bullet drop/hole physics/duds) and it gives reactions from the ships that's monitoring you (or not) a proper logical and realistic reaction to a torpedo fired at them.

But there's some unrealistic things like you can't send radio if your ship isn't fully surfaced, so you must surface fully to send radio reports and hope in these minutes of blowing the tanks, some bombers don't creep up on you. Everything else works fine through the power of mods, although if you want to observe from the periscope depth to add live blips on the map I believe the only person that can do it is you, the player.

And the best thing about all of this is the historical accuracy of the tools it gives you to enact that accuracy, all the maps, trading, mining, air recon, data are real. And the tools, charts, radio signals, orders, and the enigma machine are present and functioning in the game the same way they would in real life. I had to take about 10 minutes to learn how to use the enigma machine they had in ww2 to decipher a message because you get two of those messages even if you turned the encryption off.

If you want a submarine game this is *the one*, looks good, plays good, "Wolf of Steel" mod fixed the bugs. Only complaint is multiplayer is coop only and you can't uboat with friends on the same ship.

PS. You can receive reports of sinking ships to rescue their rafts from both sides of the war, but I always flak gun them because I'm afraid they'll snitch about the uboats to their ally destroyers and then the ocean will be covered with sonar pings and wasserbomben.

PSS. This entire game might feel like it's undertranslated due to the hefty amount of german words you will see. But watch das boot once and it'll get you in the mood.

name: Silent Hunter 5: Battle of the Atlantic
review: This game can be a lot of fun if you mod it. The simple way to do that is to get The Wolves of Steel mega mod which now contains a patcher that works with the Steam version. The mod will allow you to play the game as hardcore sim as you want but also allows easy targeting that you don't see in some of the videos on line about the game. For those who want the game in spoken English be sure to back up your steam game before modding it you will need the voice files because TWOS mod overwrites the English with German.  As for all the comments about the game being broken, well yes out of the box you will never get past the first campaign, with modding you get the whole war from 1939 on. With some of the radio and music mods that you can add you can hear BBC broadcasts that happened during the war in the years that they happened. It can be a very immersive fun game but it can be a challenge to mod  which is why TWOS mod was created.

name: Silent Hunter 5: Battle of the Atlantic
review:
Realized I never wrote a review for this game. I played this game when it first came out.. It was a steaming pile of buggy mess.. I then forgot about it and continued playing SH4. Fast forward nearly a decade. I decide to come back. Only to find.. It's still just as crappy... 

First thing most notable is the complete lack of any useful buttons or information on the UI or screens unlike SH4, but we'll get back to that.

This game focuses on Roleplaying.. Which is great.. except for the fact, they went waaaay overboard in all the wrong ways... 

First Impression....

The tutoria.. It shows you how to move on the map.. Great.. It shows you how to shoot a non moving target... ehh?.. you quickly find upon continuation of playing the game, that infact, all the targets are NOT sitting still unlike the tutorial.. how do you aim? how do you compensate for the speed? WHO KNOWS! Tutorial certainly doesn't tell you... How do you turn on the automatic aiming like SH4? well even that is convoluted, and only about 40% accurate, even on targets that aren't trying to dodge and sailing straight... 

So now I got this completely lone merchant, which my crew seems unable to hit, so I manually put in the information myself and stop wasting torpedoes... (luckily for me I know how to do it from SH4, because the tutorial again... doesn't even tell you how to fucking shoot your only real weapon derp...) Hit it once, surface.. guns wont shoot... quick google search.. ah they're supposed to shoot automatically.. naturally, but they aren't... so lets do it myself.. so I run all the way to deck gun...

Ok, merchant down.. oh but now I got contact of a destroyer closing in, better dive and get away right? click dive 5-6 times.. nothing... ok? quick search on google.. Oh! of course.. simply telling my crew to dive isn't enough, I have to manually move my captain down too.. silly me.. of course he wouldn't just go down himself like in SH4... 

Alright, that's out of the way, turn my engines low to conserve battery power... but.. where's my fuel? hmm no clue.. it's not on the UI like in SH4 readily available.. naw lets try to run through the submarine like a captain on crack trying to get in everyones face to see who can tell me the fule levels.. oh wait but that information is on the most disgustingly horrible UI I've ever seen in a game.. let me just click FOUR TIMES to get to this information.. information I'll probably need to go too many times throughout my voyage to check fuel levels... 

You know.. it's not like the captain couldn't yell for fuel level checks.. but of course that'd require #logic... 

resurface the ship, only to find once again another vitally important thing is missing.. the battery levels... and they aren't recharging... Yet ANOTHER google search, only to learn 2 things.. 1) THey are supposed to be recharging automatically (Hint.. they aren't... yet another game breaking bug one of many I decided to leave out here cuz fuck this game anyway), and 2) the battery recharges faster the slower you're going and the less engines you're using.... Geee wouldn't that of been nice to have in the tutorial? 

Wanna rig for silent running? nope not a button the UI, or anything, you gotta run around in the sub finding the right person to toggle in the crew... 

You wanna pre heat your torpedoes? Load them? Charge the batteries? DO anything of importance? naw you can't just order it from the command room like any other sane naval commander would of done, you gotta run from 1 end of the submarine to the other non stop issueing non stop orders.... Why the fuck do I even have a crew? I'm doing everything myself anyway... 

This game.. is complete garbage.... 

1) UI elements that are critically important are missing from the screens they should be one like SH4. 

2) No quick ways to issue orders easily... 

3) AI is brain dead stupid

4) littered with game breaking bugs. (5 in 5 seperate attempts at a playthrough, all within the first hour) 

5) nonsensical UI

You should be paid by Ubisoft to play this game, not the other way around.

name: Silent Hunter 5: Battle of the Atlantic
review:
Ahoi, sailor. Don't you wish you'd have a game so unfinished and broken, that you'll need to track no longer supported -- or even hosted -- mods just to have the crewmen get in the boat when you submerge? (No, seriously, I'm at 300 ft deep and I see the numbskulls on the conning tower with their binocculars through the periscope.) Don't you wish that the game would think you have a Tardis and your kills happened in month 0 of year 0, or, literal example I've seen, in month 39 of year 16183, so they don't count for a WW2 mission?

More importantly: Don't you wish you could live an accurate simulation of sailing on a big open ocean, with NOTHING HAPPENING FOR WEEKS AT A TIME? Don't you wish your time compression would be disabled every time a crew member as much as sees a fly, so it could all translate into hours of pointlessly wasting your life, just being bored out of your skull? Don't you wish you'd then have to redo the same boring patrol over and over again, because either the game crashed, or the required tonnage bar stopped updating, or some AI freighter crashed into you in port when you were coming back to hand in the quest? 

Because literally that'll be your experience when playing this game.

And for whoever is going for "but it's realistic!", so is spending half a year in training and another in a trench where nothing happens for infantrymen, but we don't make games about THOSE boring parts, do we? We skip to when shooty guy got to shoot someone, not make you live through every boring routine patrol where nothing happened.

Look, big world open games are only fun if you're going to do the Witcher 3 thing, where there might be a quest or some ruin to explore or such every hundred feet or less. Behind every rock, every bush, every bend in the road, or rickety hut door, there might be some interesting item, some quest giver, or the entrance to a secret dungeon, or just a few coins worth of loot. That's the only reason for me to run along some path in the woods like an idiot. If there is nothing for me to discover, then it's just boring, and you should let me just fast travel to my assigned patrol zone and be done with it.

And no, the time compression that keeps resetting if as much as the sonar guy thought he heard a fart is not it.

name: Silent Hunter 5: Battle of the Atlantic
review:
One of the best games I've ever played. 

played +100 hours in the first month, 100 after that, and hopefully many more to come. 

so IMERSIVE
this is the only game I know of that actually makes me feel like i'm actually in world war ll. and some basic characterization of the crew with a tone of camaraderie. and to that, the graphics are spectacular, the explosions are big and bright with debris flying across the sky. 


why this game makes all the PREVIOUS GAMES seem unplayably obsolete
being in first person AND being able to walk around your boat only adds adds further to the immersion but going back to being limited to set positions on the boat to teleport to and from becomes awkward. 

controls
the controls are so much better than previous versions. there are many improvements but most importantly. wasd > arrows, nuff said.

speaking of controls, one gripe I do have is that you really should have a FULL KEYBOARD.
If your keyboard doesn't have: delete, insert, page up and down. you cannot fully lower your periscopes (regular & tactical) making you stick out like a sore thumb while on the surface, will also force you to have to manually lower them below the water and is just annoying to know you can't do anything about it. A full numpad is also highly recommended, the numpad +, -, ., and 0 are needed for manual ship navigation and time compresion shortcuts.   

BUGS
It's not that bad. 
at first the game didn't even function, so googled a simple fix for it.go to the local game files, select the properties of the sh5 file, compatibility->change high DPI->override high DPI behavior.
There are many fixes for every issue and there is a lot of info online for every issue. Other than that launch problem, bugs are rare. Aside from the unsurprising glitches like running the game at 8192x speed is janky and the physics can occasionally be weird just like any other game from the era (It's amusing if you ask me). People have talked about the tonnage sunk counter not counting anymore, but I've not had those problems.
The game works better than people complain about it.

GAMEPLAY
assuming you like submarine games, this the peak, the top, the apex. This game has everything you could ever want in a sub sim. Even though the cycle of: search for target, prepare an ambush, torpedo, deck gun, and watch da big ka-boom, then repeat that process until you are out of torpedoes, return to dock for more torpedoes, repeat. Technically this repetitive cycle should get boring eventually, but I just can't stop having fun with it. 

among the greatest

name: Silent Hunter 5: Battle of the Atlantic
review:
Seeing the pedigree set by the previous entries in the series, the lush graphics, and the hype surrounding being able to wander your submarine, you may make the mistake of buying this dumbed-down, buggy mess. Please don't. The graphic engine makes pretty wrapping paper, but it can't mask the scent of the turd it surrounds. The complete absence of any sort of QA process becomes immediately apparent upon recieving a mission brief and being told to select a mission. There are none. After stuffing around with your saved games to fix the problem, you will be instructed to sink 100k tons of shipping off the western approaches. Sink as many ships as you like, that progress bar is never going to fill up. You will find you need to wander around in first- person to give orders to individual sailors to get things done. This seems like a fine idea, until you realise that it takes three attempts on average to use a ladder, and getting stuck is so common that the devs generously bound a default hotkey to unstick you. controls are obtuse, buttons and shortcuts aren't where you might expect them to be, your boat is crewed by potatoes who seem to cease doing anything useful as soon as they're not in arm's reach, and the attack map "system" is a disgrace. When tied up inside a U-Boat pen, your boat bucks and rolls like it's in the mid atlantic in a gale. You can stand on the wharf, under a 7m concrete roof, next to your boat in maintenance, and press hotkeys to remotely raise and lower the periscopes, while your watch on deck search tirelessly for enemy ships. inside the sub-pen. These are the kind of no brainers which should be fixed before the game hits the shelf, except it seems Ubi got greedy, and wanted a half baked AAA blockbuster in record time. Speaking of Ubi, the game forces you to use u-play, a hate-crime of a DRM system which causes more issues than it solves. The only upside of this game is the tribe of rivet counting autists who tirelessly build mods out of sheer love for the genre. SH3 and 4 were good games that could be modded into great games. SH5 is an unplayable wreck that can be modded into a playable one. the problem with mods is they will just give you a taste for the game SH5 could have been, which will fill you with a sadness which can only be cured by playing 600 hours of silent hunter 3 with the GWX mod.
TL;DR: Get SH3 and the GWX 3.0 mod, pretend SH5 never existed. It's for the best.

review:
Silent Hunter 5 - A game that should’ve been sunk the day it was released.

I only paid the price of a cup of coffee for this steaming pile of crap, thank FK for that.
The Wolves of Steel made it playable but only on the ‘terms’ of the german creators that think the game ‘must’ be made so hard that it’s borderline unplayable unless you consider yourself an elitist ‘over-the-top’ simulator enthusiast that must have every bell and whistle available. And the never ending German talking/shouting is just too much for me to take anymore, it’s nauseating!

The graphics don’t seem much better than SH4, nor the gameplay.
The textures on the highest level are washed out and the physics are a mess.
The ‘TWoS’ FIX/addon was a nightmare to download from some basement in the middle of Eastern FK’n nowhere, then a hell of a lot of reading to even get it to work, but I did of course, and it seems all for nothing as I hated this game and it’s elitist gameplay style. (Oh and no, I don’t give a TOSS what any fan boi’s have to say to that! If this game floats your boat - here’s your paddle, now FK off)

On a side note, and a good one,,, I do regard Silent Hunter 4 as a top shelf simulator that can be toned down, both with the ‘wailing’ German speech and difficulty levels, plus expansions and skins etc to last a good while out on the ocean dodging tommie destroyers.

Ignore this SH5 garbage, which btw, should’ve been removed from Steam a long time ago as no one likes it and almost everyone seeks a refund after realising the game is faulty and needs an external patch to even see it FFS!

name: Silent Hunter 5: Battle of the Atlantic
review: [u] Straight from installation: [/u] is SH5 playable? Yes, it is to an extent. [u] Is it stable? [/u] Yes, it runs just fine on windows 10. [b] However [/b] many inconcistencies exist gameplaywise which may as well make this game the same as any other CTD riddled mess [i] (such as the ship recognition interface being written with completely incorrect dimensions regarding ship mast heights, draft depths, vessel lengths and beam (width)) [/i]. There exist certain coding issues [i] (while rare, a possibility for a bug making all torpedoes fired by the player treated as duds, regardless of the relevant gameplay option being enabled or disabled) [/i]. Another possibility exists that may bar the player from ever progressing beyond the starting campaign begining in 1939. Now, these are [u] only possibilities [/u] and [u] not always encountered, [/u] but these have been encountered often enough to come to the attention of many. There exist many mods that fix many of these issues and enhance gameplay. Hell, I couldnt stand this game until downloading and installing a megamod hosted by the subsim community known as The Wolves Of Steel. [u] So: do I reccommend this game right out of the box? [/u] [i] Absolutely not. [/i] After modding the inherrent bugs, glitches, and recursive coding issues out of existence? Shit yeah! This becomes an excellent Uboot simulator after fixing it up, but patience is a definite must played vanilla or modded

name: Silent Hunter 5: Battle of the Atlantic
review:
I really wanted to like this game, but I just couldn't for two major reasons:
1. After playing the campaign for about 12 hours, I got to a spot where whatever I do (besides loading an older save) crashes the game. So, lots of my campaign progress has been lost.
2. There are tons of bugs in the game, some minor, some not so minor. For example, sometimes sounds just stop working for no good reason, sometimes functionality just stops working (e.g., depth under keel). And, oh... the periscope view using the default periscope depth is next to useless as most times the periscope is under water... ugh.

Add to all that the just completely useless and stupid Ubisoft launcher (why can't I just lanuch from Steam! in 2018?) and I have given up on this game. I really enjoyed the idea and some of the gameplay, but the game is just a mess.

OK, so maybe I should have gotten the Wolves of Steel mega-mod (which requies an account on subsim), but really I need to mod the heck out of the game to make it functional?

name: Silent Hunter 5: Battle of the Atlantic
review:
Ok. The ghame is old, but still ...

Let's start with the annoying stuff.
The game doesn't save your settings, like the size/poision of the minimap or the tutorial tips for some controls will pop up EVERY single time you start/load a game or even when entering/leaving the port.
Even though the game only has like a dozen hotkeys assigned, those are spread all over the keyboard and no ... you cannot change them. There is only a "help" in game showing a US layout keyboard with where the hotkeys are ... good luck if you don't have the US layout or no keypad...
You have to travel thousands of miles, for that you have time compression. But you have to hit the time compression button a dozen times to have any useful value and any spotted ship/plane/shallow waters/ a seagull shitting on your conning tower will completely reset it ... nothing you can do about it. Even worse you set waypoints ... then die because your boat beached on a 1m² island because the nonexistent AI doesn't warn nor bother evading. Naturally that "island" can only be seen if you completely zoom in to the map, so everytime you set a 20000 miles course you have to waste 30minutes checking every inch of the course with max zoom to make sure there isn't a pile of dirt on the course.
Talking about AI ... i have yet to see hostile planes do anything and i have yet to see any hostile ship cause any damage except for firing his main guns while i'm surfaced...
As you play your Crew members can unlock abilities, including active ones. But in order to activate the ability you actually have to walk to the officer and talk to him...
You can manually fire the deck gun and AA. Manning the AA is easy, manning the deck gun not. You cannot leave the conning tower and the tower blocks your line of sight to the gun. So you have to maniacally move your mouse around praying for the 2 visible pixel of the barrel to be highlighted indicating that you can man the gun with leftclick...

Now for the for gamebreaking stuff.
When i started the game it automatically closed without any notice. Ok, I'm using Win10 64Bit and the game is old ... some searching the web i found out i have to manually edit some settings ... too bad the posts describing that are basically as old as the game itself.
Even though the game now runs, it still occasionally closes without any warning or notice.
Hotkeys, especially the ones for manning the deck gun / AA tend to not work at all. You actually have to walk to the conning tower and talk to the officer to get the guns mounted, unmounting the guns via hotkey works though...
Even though the savegame directly lists special ships and their status (active/sunk) it doesn't work, by now i have sunk the HMS Rodney, HMS Queen Elizabeth and HMS Hood at least 4 times in a single mission...
On my first trip to the port of Dover there was some dozen ship wrecks happily skipping on the water surface ...
Every one in a while when you surface your deck officer (and the spotters) refuse to man the conning tower ... which means you can't talk to him to man the guns ... only way to fix it is by restarting the game.
Winning missions is nearly impossible, for qhatever stupid reason kills are not contributed to mission objectives. Only way to "fix" it is by manually editing your save file.


Various posts and threads confirm that those problems and bugs have been around since release. So it heavily looks like Ubisoft just sold a beta and abandoned it right away.
On a sidenote there are mods, supposedly fixing a good amount of things. I didn't try them out. For me a game that requires some dozen mods to be playable,let alone enjoyable, is just crap ... especially if the game doesn't even directly support modding.

If you want a sub game, just play it's predecessors ... or different games. Silent Hunter 5 is garbage ... at best

name: Silent Hunter 5: Battle of the Atlantic
review:
EDITED REVIEW


EDIT:  I don't know what part of me said to give it a shot, but I shouldn't have listened to it.  The game simply is too buggy to enjoy.  Case in point:  One of the missions starting out is to find an aircraft carrier patroling the atlantic and sink it.  No big deal right?  So I go out on search for this thing, two in game months of false contacts and enemy patrols that don't consist of this thing go by and nothing.  Finally I see it, crusing away at twice the speed my sub can move off into the distance.  A while later I see it again, this time closing on me.  I launch every torpedo I have at this thing and get 2 hits, the health bar is low and the flooding bar is filling up, I've done it.  Then the flooding just stops.  I wait out the destroyers and surface.  It's listing and has water over the flight deck, but not sinking.  I fire every one of my 88 shells at it and not a scratch, there it floats.  I make a note of where it is and go sail to the closest resupply spot and go back to finish it once and for all.  It's gone.





The controls are completely different from pervious games and you have to pretty much fumble around yourself to learn them.  I can't even get past the first mission in which you have to sink an aircraft carrier, I saw it once and couldn't catch up to it.  I sailed around for a month looking for it but it was never seen again.  Oh, and multiplayer is no longer available.  

All that being said give it a shot, I gave it one last chance after reviewing, and it does a lot of stuff right.  You can walk around your sub from front to back, customize your sub in more ways than before.  And the graphics are acceptable on even the most basic level.  As for the controls yes they're weird at first, but give it a half hour and you'll get the hang of it.  I think my first review was too harsh. i give it 7.9/10

Another "game" sh@ out by Ubisoft. IDK how products like these still manage to be on Steam.

First of all, the game doesn't even work for some people (especially those with Windows 10). I was lucky enough to have it working on my pc but damn, it's still terrible. The settings are pretty much useless because once you launch the campaign, it hovers at around 8-12fps. For reference, my graphics card is a GTX1070 paired with an i7-7700hq so I was expecting a game from 2010 to run without problems.
I even tried to lower the settings and all it did was make the game look worse and gave me 1 extra fps. Definitely not recommended to buy this floating turd of a game because to even play the game, you need to be a computer science major while also learning how to tinker with the game's files which shouldn't even be happening since this is a paid game.

If you are looking at this review right now and wondering if you should pay for this game, the answer is a big fat NO. Games like these don't deserve money and instead deserve to be pirated because there is literally no support from the devs and multiplayer is almost dead anyway.

name: Silent Hunter 5: Battle of the Atlantic
review:
I like to think im a frugle spender. The first silent hunter game I bought was 'Silent Hunter 3' for around £3-£4. I didnt expect much from an old, cheap game but, My God, was I impressed! for a game that came out when I was 7 its beautiful. I then bought the package for around £25. I imediatly installed 'Silent Hunter 4' and was again blown away!

 Then came 'Silent Hunter 5'. Its not just a game or a Simulator. Its a window into the lives of the crews of the Submarines of World War 2! 
Each of the main crew members has a back story. They all have their own reasons for joining the Kreigsmarine. They are'nt all raving, racist Nazis (bar one). They're all human, and you can really identify with many of them. 

The Graphics are excellent and the gameplay is so realistic it blows my mind. I've had to actually use the maths I spent most of my childhood learning at school to play this game. And that was only optional! 

If you're a history nerd like me and are interested in the human side of WW2 as well as being a Submarine lover then buy this game!
